title: Director, Data Science & Analytics
description: The Opportunity Adobe Firefly is one of the most significant bets in Adobe's history — our generative AI platform that is redefining what creativity looks like for millions of creators and businesses worldwide. This role will be helping shape one of the most strategically important initiatives across the entire company. We are seeking a world-class Director of Data Science & Analytics to be the analytical engine behind Firefly's creator growth strategy within the Creator org. You will work at the intersection of AI product, product marketing, and advanced data science to shape how Firefly acquires , engages, and retains the next generation of creators. This is a high-visibility, high-impact Individual Contributor role with direct and regular engagement with Adobe's C-suite and senior leadership. Your insights will be central in executive briefings, inform product roadmaps, and influence decisions that shape the future of generative AI at Adobe. If you are a n experienced data scientist who thrives on high impact work, wants to operate at the frontier of AI and creativity, and is energized by influencing strategy at the highest levels of a Fortune 500 company, this role was built for you. What You'll Do Drive Firefly's Growth Intelligence by serving as the definitive analytical voice for Firefly's creator growth , by defining the metrics, models, and frameworks that track Firefly's trajectory and inform its most critical strategic decisions. Regularly present insights, forecasts, and strategic recommendations directly to Adobe's senior leadership and C-suite. Translate complex analytical findings into clear, compelling narratives that drive executive decision-making on Firefly's biggest opportunities and risks. Champion a culture of analytical rigor across the Firefly org. Define standards, introduce new methodologies, and inspire teams to make better decisions through data. Develop and refine customer segmentation models to identify behavioral clusters and inform targeting strategies. Build and maintain forecasting models to predict demand and user engagement trends. Define and exemplify the value of customers over and develop strategies to improve it in acquisition and retention processes. Bring rigorous data science to Firefly's go-to-market planning and campaign execution. Identify competitive gaps, market opportunities, and return on investment levers that help Firefly win in a fast-moving generative AI landscape. Lead churn modeling initiatives to proactively identify at-risk users and recommend mitigation strategies. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design experiments and interpret results, including A/B testing and cohort analyses. Share complex modeling outcomes and insights with both technical and non-technical collaborators. Why This Role You will work on Adobe's #1 strategic priority. Firefly is central to Adobe's future, and your work will be central to Firefly's success. You will have a direct line to the top. This role carries significant executive visibility. Expect your work to regularly reach and directly influence Adobe's most senior leaders. You will operate at the frontier. Shaping the future of Firefly , and Adobe more broadly, puts you at the intersection of generative AI, creator culture, and global-scale innovation . What You Need to Succeed 1 2 + years of experience in data science, advanced analytics, or a related field, ideally with significant exposure to consumer product or AI-driven businesses. A track record of operating at a senior individual contributor level , known for the depth and quality of your analytical work, and for influencing strategy well beyond your immediate team. Demonstrated experience presenting to and influencing senior executive audiences with data-driven insights and recommendations. Deep passion for the creator economy; you go deep to understand creators across a wide range of segments and personas. Strong proficiency in Python and SQL for data manipulation, modeling, and analysis. Expertise in data visualization tools like Tableau or equivalent. Strong foundation in statistical modeling, experimentation, and lifecycle analytics. Exceptional communication and storytelling skills — you can translate the most complex model outputs into narratives that move executives to action. This senior-level individual contributor will own a defined portfolio of content focused on key product value propositions, real-world workflows, and use cases aligned to product and GTM priorities to drive meaningful conversations across target online communities. Beyond campaign execution, this role will evolve and optimize the operating systems that power community content at scale. You will elevate the voice of the community into strategic decision-making, strengthen agency partnerships, and drive continuous improvement across how content is planned, produced, and measured. You will act as both strategic architect and hands-on producer, shaping scalable content programs that deepen community engagement and translate content interaction into active product use. Translate strategic objectives and product value propositions into scalable full-funnel community marketing content that drives sharing, conversation, and perception shifts within key online creator communities. Drive continuous improvement in production workflows, finding opportunities to increase efficiency and quality. Lead and optimize relationships with external agency partners (paid amplification and video production), defining scopes of work and performance expectations to improve throughput and impact. Voice of the Community & Strategic Influence Establish structured feedback loops that synthesize creator and community sentiment across campaigns on a weekly basis. Translate qualitative insights and performance data into actionable recommendations that influence messaging, positioning, and GTM priorities. Serve as a strategic connector between community signals and broader marketing decision-making. Elevate the voice of the community to inform messaging, positioning, and marketing narratives that resonate within target online communities. Performance & Optimization Define Key performance and success metrics across awareness, engagement, and product usage stages. Drive paid media campaigns, manage agency relationship, and leverage findings to inform future optimizations. Analyze quantitative and qualitative performance data to optimize campaign and content effectiveness. Drive measurable improvements in engagement, sentiment, and product adoption through structured testing and iteration. Strengthen insight into content performance through standardized reporting frameworks. What You'll Need to Succeed 10+ years of experience in marketing, integrated content strategy, community marketing, or media strategy for creators or artists. Strong understanding of how online communities, platform algorithms, and content formats influence sharing behavior and conversation dynamics. Proven success owning full-funnel content portfolios tied to measurable engagement outcomes. Demonstrated experience scaling high-volume content programs within complex, matrixed organizations. Experience evolving content systems, workflows, and operational frameworks to increase output and efficiency. Strong track record managing agency partners and improving performance and production output. Expertise in using community driven content to inspire action and deepen product engagement. You will work closely with the Chief Accounting Officer’s chief of staff and leadership team to turn ideas into action. Clear and confident communicator with excellent writing and presentation skills with experience crafting and delivering communications at the executive level. Strong and creative problem-solver who brings new ideas and drives continuous improvement. A thoughtful, steady, and collaborative approach; able to work with a wide range of personalities. Adaptability in a fast-paced setting and ability to lead through ambiguity. Ability to work independently and integrate within a team. title: Director, Content Operations
description: The opportunity Adobe is seeking a Director of Content Operations to shape how ideas become exceptional content across our global marketing organization. This leader will build and run the operational model that powers how creative work enters, moves through, and scales across Adobe’s Global Marketing content supply chain. This role drives the processes and planning that align marketing demand with production capacity across the organization, including intake, prioritization, capacity planning, and workflow management. In partnership with in-house creative teams and agency partners, the Director ensures work is delivered predictably, efficiently, and at scale. What you’ll do Lead the operational model that governs how work moves through the content production pipeline. Establish intake and prioritization governance that translates marketing demand into clear, prioritized production work. Forecast demand and align work with available capacity across internal creative teams and AOR partners. Define workflows, service levels, and operating guidelines that accelerate production across campaigns, channels, and formats. Establish operational governance and review cadences to track capacity, SLA performance, agency efficiency, and delivery outcomes. Oversee agency operating models, including workflow integration, engagement standards, and performance expectations. Lead agency contract renewals working closely with Procurement and executive partners, evaluating performance and guiding renewal or partner selection decisions. Partner with Content Technology, Standards & Enablement, Content Intelligence, and Strategic Operations to align workflows with platforms, enablement programs, and insights. Define operational metrics with the Content Intelligence team to identify friction points and improve efficiency across the content supply chain. Success criteria 10+ years of experience in marketing operations, creative operations, production operations, or content supply chain roles. Demonstrated success designing scalable workflows supporting complex marketing or creative environments involving internal teams and agencies. Experience managing agency partnerships or vendor ecosystems. Strong systems-thinking mindset with the ability to translate complexity into clear operational frameworks. Proven ability to align cross-functional partners across creative, marketing, agency, and technology teams. Experience leading teams responsible for defining and enforcing operational processes, prioritization of resources, or production coordination. Exceptional communication and stakeholder management skills in highly matrixed organizations. title: Digital Strategist
description: About the Role As an Digital Strategist , you will play a pivotal role in ensuring the successful adoption of marketing technology platforms by increasing employee engagement, streamlining operational models, and driving sustainable change. You'll work with Adobe’s largest enterprise customers, guiding them through digital marketing transformation across technology, business processes, organizational structures, and workforce capabilities. This role requires a combination of strategic thinking, stakeholder management, and hands-on experience with organizational change. You’ll help customers navigate the complexities of onboarding and adopting new digital solutions—ensuring they unlock the full potential of their investments. Key Responsibilities Strategy & Change Leadership Lead digital transformation engagements focused on change management, operating model refinement, and organizational design. Partner with executive stakeholders to align digital strategy with business goals. Facilitate discovery sessions and strategic workshops to uncover needs and co-create solutions. Deliver compelling executive-level presentations, strategic roadmaps, and value frameworks. Change Management Execution Apply structured change management methodologies (e.g., Prosci) to drive adoption. Create actionable deliverables for change levers: communication plans, sponsor roadmaps, training and coaching programs, and resistance management. Assess change readiness, conduct impact analyses, and develop stakeholder engagement plans. Support project teams in integrating change activities into delivery plans. Organizational Enablement Define and implement future-state operating models, team structures, and process frameworks. Coach senior leaders and managers to act as effective change sponsors. Support the development and delivery of training programs to drive user adoption. Practice Development Contribute to the evolution of the Digital Strategy & Performance practice. Share knowledge, mentor team members, and support methodology development. Collaborate closely with Adobe’s delivery and partner teams to ensure seamless execution. Required Skills & Qualifications 10+ years of experience in digital strategy, marketing technology, or business transformation. Proven success leading large-scale change and adoption programs. Strong executive presence and communication skills—verbal, written, and visual. Experience with Adobe Experience Cloud or similar platforms (Salesforce, Oracle, etc.). Deep understanding of digital marketing strategy and marketing operations. Background in one or more key industries (e.g., retail, finance, travel, media). Hands-on experience in creating and delivering value-driven business cases. Exceptional problem-solving, analytical, and stakeholder management capabilities. Preferred Certifications Change Management: Prosci Change Management Certification APMG International Change Management Certification Project & Portfolio Management: Familiarity with project management tools, phases, and methodologies Additional Attributes Naturally collaborative and comfortable working in cross-functional environments. Resilient, adaptable, and able to work through ambiguity. Organized with a strategic mindset and tactical execution skills. Strong influencing skills and ability to drive consensus among diverse stakeholders. Passion for helping clients achieve measurable business value through transformation. title: Software Development Engineer, Influencer Management
description: The Opportunity This is a position where you will build and launch a new product within Adobe working within an exciting org that is incubating next-generation ideas. Lead a team of engineers, designers, and prototypers to bring to life a product, test it with customers and demonstrate product-market fit. This role is an outstanding opportunity to operate like a startup founder while having the resources of an innovative, large Technology company. Join us for an opportunity to invent new technologies and supply a brand new and existing Adobe products. The Challenge We are looking for a Full Stack Engineer to build a next-generation platform. This platform enables enterprise brands and creators to collaborate on influencer marketing initiatives from start to finish. Work in a fast-moving incubator team , will work across the stack to build core product capabilities, helping translate opportunities in the creator economy into scalable, high-quality software. C ollaborate closely with product managers, designers, and engineers to deliver features quickly while maintaining enterprise-grade standards for scalability, reliability, and security . A core part of the platform will be the use of Agentic AI systems to streamline and automate the complex workflows involved in influencer marketing — from discovery and contract management to campaign execution, analytics, and optimization. Implement AI-native product experiences by integrating LLM-powered services and processes into the platform. Looking for someone who enjoys building new products from the ground up , taking ownership of complex problems, and working in a highly collaborative, flat team environment . E xperiment with emerging AI capabilities, and play a key role in bringing a new product to life within Adobe’s incubator ecosystem. Responsibilities Compose, build, and scale full-stack product capabilities that power an enterprise platform for managing influencer projects end-to-end. Develop backend services and support workflows such as creator discovery, campaign planning, collaboration, contract management, performance tracking, and analytics . Implement AI-powered features that streamline influencer marketing operations, including creator matching, campaign optimization, content insights, and workflow automation. Integrate LLMs and agentic AI systems to support intelligent workflows across the platform, including task orchestration, recommendation systems, and automated campaign assistance. Build scalable data pipelines and integrations with external platforms and APIs such as social networks, analytics tools, and marketing systems. Develop intuitive, performant front-end experiences that allow enterprise teams to manage campaigns, collaborate with creators, and analyze performance insights. Ensure systems are reliable, performant, secure, and scalable to meet enterprise product requirements. Contribute to observability, monitoring, and evaluation frameworks that ensure AI driven features behave reliably and transparently for enterprise users. What is needed to succeed 8+ years of professional experience developing full-stack web applications. Strong proficiency with JavaScript/TypeScript, React, and Node.js (or comparable frameworks). Experience designing and building scalable APIs, backend services, and modern web architectures . Experience integrating AI services, LLM APIs, or machine learning capabilities into production applications. Familiarity with data modeling, integrations, and working with external platforms or third-party services . Strong problem-solving and debugging skills, with the ability to work across frontend, backend, and system integrations . Excellent communication and collaboration skills; thrives in fast-paced, multi-functional environments . Familiarity with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP and modern deployment practices. title: Marketing Manager, India Student - Design & Video
description: Marketing Manager, India Student - Design & Video Based in Noida/Bangalore Our Company Changing the world through digital experiences is what Adobe’s all about. We give everyone—from emerging artists to global brands—everything they need to design and deliver exceptional digital experiences! We’re passionate about empowering people to create beautiful and powerful images, videos, and apps, and transform how companies interact with customers across every screen. We’re on a mission to hire the very best and are committed to creating exceptional employee experiences where everyone is respected and has access to equal opportunity. We realize that new ideas can come from everywhere in the organization, and we know the next big idea could be yours! Role Overview Adobe's Student Engagement Marketing team exists to encourage the long-term use of Adobe's tools with students and universities, with a particular focus on embedding Adobe Firefly and AI-powered workflows into the everyday practices of video and design students. We’re looking for a highly creative Marketing Specialist to build, manage and energise our student evangelist presence across key design and film schools in the India. This person will identify, grow, and nurture a passionate group of design & video students at some of the most prestigious universities in the country to champion Adobe’s brand and tools, and build usage of our latest technology with their peer groups. This role is suited to someone immersed in creative culture, with a deep understanding of the motivations and challenges of design and video students. It’s a highly hands‑on role - you’ll spend real time with students, listening, advising, and being present in their creative world! title: Digital Employee Experience Analyst
description: Opportunity We are building a modern, data‑driven Digital Employee Experience (DEX) capability focused on improving how employees interact with workplace technology. As a DEX Analyst, you will use Nexthink and experience analytics to measure, understand, and elevate the digital experiences of thousands of employees. This role blends technical skill, human‑centered thinking, and analytic storytelling to identify friction, drive improvements, and influence how our workforce engages with devices, applications, and support systems. Responsibilities Nexthink Platform & Telemetry Administer Nexthink modules, dashboards, campaigns, investigations, and automated remediations. Build experience scorecards aligned to organizational priorities. Create visualizations, alerts, and reports that translate telemetry into actionable insights. Ensure data quality, tagging accuracy, and proper configuration across hardware, OS, and applications. Experience Analytics & Insights Analyze performance, stability, sentiment, and behavior patterns to identify trends and root causes. Combine quantitative and qualitative signals to understand true employee impact. Translate complex technical findings into clear, compelling narratives for leadership and engineering teams. Form hypotheses, test improvements, and measure effects on experience scores. Human‑Centered Improvement Understand employee behaviors, work styles, and friction patterns to recommend improvements. Partner with support, engineering, cybersecurity, and product teams to design user‑focused solutions. Use Nexthink to support surveys, feedback loops, and engagement strategies. Advocate for end‑users by ensuring technical decisions reflect human experience implications. Operational Excellence Participate in incident analysis to assess user impact. Monitor technology performance trends to proactively reduce friction and prevent recurring issues. Support DEX roadmap initiatives to expand automation, maturity, and long‑term capability. What is needed to succeed: 1-2 years of hands‑on experience with Nexthink (administration, investigations, dashboards, campaigns, automated remediation, scoring). 4-10 years of exp within a Data Analysis role Solid understanding of Windows and/or macOS environments and endpoint performance metrics. Strong analytical skills working with large datasets. Ability to translate complex data into simple, human‑centered insights. Superb communication and collaboration skills. Passion for user empathy and understanding digital behaviors. Supported organizations with 15k+ devices Preferred Qualifications 2+ years in DEX, EUC/endpoint engineering, IT operations, or related fields. Scripting experience (PowerShell, Python). Familiarity with ITSM/ITIL practices. Experience with telemetry, observability, or experience‑measurement platforms. Knowledge of survey development or UX research. title: Lead Forward Deployed Creative Technologist
description: The Opportunity Every global brand is now a content factory. They need thousands of on-brand asset variations every quarter — static, video, and increasingly 3D — and they need to ship them in days, not weeks. The customer who figures this out first wins the next decade of marketing. We are the team that gets them there. We embed with enterprise customers, learn their content supply chain end-to-end, and craft custom AI-powered creative workflows on top of Adobe Firefly Services and Firefly Creative Production (our node-based workflow builder). We are not pre-sales engineers. We are not consultants. We are technical creators who ship . You will own the bridge between what the customer's CMO sketched on a whiteboard and what their creative team actually uses on Monday morning. Then you'll bring what you learned back so the next FDCT moves faster. This role is for someone who is maniacally focused on customer value . If the customer cannot point to time saved, dollars earned, or campaigns shipped because of your work, nothing else matters. What you'll do Embed with enterprise customers. Learn their brand, their creative team, their content supply chain, and the metrics their CMO cares about. Become a trusted advisor in 90 days . Translate a customer brief into a working creative workflow. Compose Firefly Services APIs (Firefly, Photoshop, InDesign, Illustrator, Express, Lightroom, Substance 3D, Audio/Video) and Workflow Builder nodes into something that actually runs . Build Figma plugins or design-system integrations when the customer's creative team works there. Build thin front-ends (or conversational experiences) and light back-ends (App Builder, Azure Functions, AWS Lambda). Ship a working URL the customer uses this quarter, not a PowerPoint. Build custom workflow nodes when existing ones fall short. Push reusable ones upstream. Integrate with the customer's stack — AEM Assets, Workfront, Frame.io, their DAM, their PIM, their brand portal. Negotiate auth, debug API quirks, own the edge cases. Train the customer's team. Document. Hand off cleanly so the customer self-serves when you rotate to the next engagement. Demo your work yourself — script, produce, edit. Show real creative outcomes, not abstract slides. Feed the org. Reusable patterns become shared repo templates, workflow templates, or upstream WFB nodes. Every engagement makes the next one faster. What you'll bring 5–10 years straddling creative production AND technical building. You have shipped both: real creative work (motion, design, video, 3D, photography, brand systems) AND real technical work (apps, integrations, plugins, automation). Creative content supply chain fluency. You understand how briefs become assets, how brand systems and design tokens work, how master files become channel variants, how creative teams hand off through Figma / PSD / INDD to DAM and review-and-approval. This is the skill we cannot teach and the one we filter hardest for. Firefly Services + Workflow Builder fluency — or evidence you ramp on a new API surface in days, not months. Adobe ecosystem depth (AEM, Workfront, Frame.io, App Builder) is a strong plus, as is Figma plugin / API fluency. Vibe-coding with judgment. You use Cursor / Claude Code / Copilot daily and ship fast. You also know when AI output is wrong, when error handling is missing, when the prompt needs more context. Production code, not prototype-only. Extreme ownership and a GSD attitude. When the integration breaks before a CMO demo, you fix it — you don't escalate. You own outcomes end-to-end, from broken auth to a missed brand-compliance check. You ship ugly first and refuse to let process slow you down. Extreme customer orientation. You hold the room with CMOs, creative directors, and heads of IT. You hear a customer pain and respond with a credible, impact-focused, creative solution — not a science experiment . You translate tech to ROI without losing the engineers in the room. A great nose for the money. You instinctively connect what you build to business outcomes the customer's exec sponsor cares about — time-to-activation, cost per variant, brand-compliance pass rate, campaign velocity. A passion for coding and tinkering. A deep interest in generative AI and creative workflows. You explore adjacent orchestration tools — ComfyUI for visual workflow prototyping, n8n / Zapier / Power Automate for low-code connectors, LangGraph / LangChain for agentic flows — because that's how you stay sharp. description: Senior Financial Analyst, Cloud COE Adobe is the leader in moving from traditional software delivery to cloud delivery. This role will be at the forefront of Adobe’s cloud growth and how Adobe inspects cloud spend. This is an exciting opportunity to join the Finance FP&A team supporting Adobe’s Cloud Operations Team. This role requires FP&A and analytical skills to administer a complex but highly strategic spend. You will work cross-functionally to bring the data points and story together. The successful candidate will engage cross-functionally across Finance, Cloud engineering to build analytical frameworks, introduce and deliver scalable reporting, and drive insights to various leadership teams. High attention to details with excellent organizational skills and ability to take charge, set objectives, drive to results. 5+ years of Finance or related experience. Bachelor's degree in business, finance or STEM fields. MBA preferred but not required. Creative, adaptive, and eager to learn new ways to accomplish complex projects. Forecasting, Governance & Collaborator Management Own a rolling multi-quarter renewal forecast, including attrition risk, upside, and execution timelines. Lead regular renewal health reviews with sales leadership, advancing at-risk renewals and competitive situations when required. Act as a trusted leader for the region, inspiring change, process adoption, and continuous improvement in retention performance. What You Bring Experience Proven experience leading sales teams in a B2B SaaS or subscription environment. Strong background in complex enterprise deals, commercial negotiations, and forecast management. Proven ability to work effectively within matrixed organizations, collaborating with Sales, Customer Success, and Services. Skills & Attributes Strong people leader with a mentoring approach and high accountability standards. Data-driven and operationally rigorous, with the ability to translate insights into action. Excellent executive communication skills, able to influence across senior collaborators. Comfortable balancing customer advocacy with commercial rigor. Why Join Play a critically important role in protecting and growing recurring revenue. Guide teams that are central to customer relationships and sustained value. Build and mature a strategic retention model across a diverse and complex region. Work with experienced sales and customer leaders across Western EMEA. title: Global Agency Lead WPP – Director Partner Sales
description: Global Agency Lead WPP – Director Partner Sales This role serves as the General Manager of one of Adobe’s most strategic global agency alliances , WPP, with end-to-end accountability for strategy, growth, and business performance . Operating as the senior-most leader for the partnership, this executive will define and execute a multi-year vision to scale revenue, expand market influence, and drive co-innovation across Adobe’s cross-cloud ecosystem . The GM will own the alliance as a business—shaping joint investments, orchestrating global go-to-market (GTM), and ensuring measurable outcomes across pipeline, bookings, and customer success. This leader will act as the primary executive interface between Adobe and the partner, aligning C-suite stakeholders and mobilizing global teams to deliver differentiated solutions and sustained growth. This is a highly visible leadership role requiring a combination of strategic thinking, commercial rigor, and executive influence , with the ability to operate across a complex, matrixed global organization. title: Sr. GTM Program Manager

This role is at the center of the action, bringing together the many teams that are a part of this effort to deliver results. This is an opportunity for an operational leader to make a huge impact on one of Adobe's flagship products. Adobe is seeking a Senior GTM Program Manager to join the Document Cloud Business Operations & Program Management team. This team drives thought leadership, planning, decision, and execution processes for the Document Product Group in partnership with a broad set of partner organizations (Product Marketing, Product Management, Go to Market, Design, Product-led Growth, Finance, corporate marketing , and more). description: The Opportunity Our DevOps team is part of the Adobe Video organization, building video creation and editing experiences for storytellers across desktop, mobile, and web. We're looking for a Mobile DevOps Engineer who is passionate about automation, continuous integration, and smooth delivery workflows. You'll collaborate closely with iOS and Android engineers to maintain and improve our CI/CD systems, streamline release processes, and ensure our tooling empowers developers to ship with confidence. Do you love building the infrastructure that lets developers deliver great mobile experiences? We do too! We are looking for someone who can implement and improve automation for mobile app building, signing, testing, and deployment. Together, we support release operations across App Store Connect and Google Play Console managing build submissions, release tracks, and metadata automation. What You'll Do You'll own the design, maintenance, and optimization of mobile test automation pipelines using modern frameworks across Android and iOS. You'll develop CI/CD workflows that automate unit, UI, functional, and performance tests, ensuring rapid feedback loops for engineering teams — and you'll monitor pipeline performance, tackle build failures, and address flaky tests before they become problems. Beyond the pipelines, you'll take ownership of build artifacts, signing certificates, provisioning profiles, and mobile telemetry integrations using Firebase and Sentry. You'll also work closely with QA and mobile engineers to establish test strategies and contribute to documentation that keeps the entire team aligned on our CI/CD processes and release flows. Ideal Qualifications We'd love to hear from you if you have: • 5-8 years of experience in DevOps, build engineering, or mobile development • A passion for improving developer productivity tooling • Practical experience with release automation tooling for iOS and Android such as: Fastlane, Gradle tasks, or custom scripting • Hands-on experience with GitHub and TeamCity, including pipeline configuration and troubleshooting • Understanding of mobile CI best practices, including test automation, dependency management, and reproducible builds • Strong communication skills and a collaborative attitude for working across engineering and release teams • Experience managing App Store Connect and Google Play Console workflows • Exposure to mobile build systems, including Xcode build tools and Android Gradle builds • Familiarity with mobile signing workflows, including certificates, key stores, provisioning profiles, and secure credential management • Familiarity with containerization and build infrastructure scaling Why Adobe Video? We offer a small-team environment with big ownership — you'll work at the intersection of world-class creative software and AI-augmented engineering. We invest in our people with industry-leading benefits, including comprehensive health coverage, generous equity, flexible time off, and access to Adobe's full suite of creative tools. We believe diverse perspectives make our products better, and we're committed to building a team where everyone can do the best work of their careers. Are you ready to shape tools used by millions of creators worldwide? We'd love to hear from you! title: Senior Data Scientist, Product Analytics
description: The Adobe Digital Imaging Data Science Team is looking for a Senior Data Scientist passionate about data and delivering an outstanding product experience. We use extensive product usage data and work closely with product managers, marketing managers, and software engineers to derive insights and build a foundation for reliable data-driven decision-making. You will focus on initiatives like product analytics, dashboards, data engineering, GTM analytics, growth and more. Your main role is to develop and maintain a strong data architecture that enhances reporting, analytics, and experimentation. Over time, you will apply predictive modeling and machine learning to help the organization improve customer experiences. Join us and drive meaningful change in Adobe’s most exciting areas! Responsibilities: Partner with product and engineering teams to understand existing product instrumentation and help bridge gaps in data streams to assist data science initiatives. Analyze product usage patterns to better understand customer behavior including acquisition, engagement, conversion, and retention. Proactively identify user trends and communicate relevant insights to assist product decision-making. Automate and optimize data pipelines using SQL and/or Python-based ETL Frameworks. Build and maintain various dashboards to inform the team about the state of the business, as well as to alert business partners when issues occur. Architect and implement models to recommend personalized in-app content. Drive A/B and multivariate tests and build feature-level experiments to validate hypotheses and influence product development decisions. Requirements: MS or Ph.D. in data science, computer science, statistics, applied mathematics, engineering, or economics, or equivalent experience. 5 - 7+ years of relevant data science experience. Experience translating business questions into data analytics approaches. Strong proficiency in querying and manipulating large datasets using SQL-like languages (Hive, Spark, etc.). Experience developing and operationalizing consistent approaches to experimentation, using appropriate statistical techniques to reduce bias and interpret statistical significance. Proficiency with descriptive and inferential statistics (i.e., t-test, chi-square, ANOVA, correlation, regression, etc.) to understand customer engagement and generate hypotheses. Experience crafting data visualizations and storytelling to efficiently communicate analysis results to both technical and non-technical audiences. Knowledge of relevant tools in this field such as Hadoop, Hive, Splunk, Spark, Tableau, Excel (Charting and Pivot-Tables), and Power BI. Experience in product instrumentation is a plus. Possess natural curiosity and technical competence, being capable of asking critical questions and always ready to address any challenges. Experience addressing an executive level audience. Excellent communication, relationship skills, and a strong teammate. title: Business Professional Enterprise Sales Specialist
description: The opportunity From the moment you wake up in the morning until you go to bed at night consider the media you consume, the adverts you see, the apps you use, the websites you browse and almost all of the shopping you do online throughout the day. Chances are that every single one of those interactions, every single one of those experiences, was touched by an Adobe product. We have a fantastic opportunity for a Business Professional Enterprise Sales Specialist to join our team based in Paris . The Business Professional Enterprise Sales Specialist is a sales professional with experience in software solution sales, preferably in the document and digital workplace space and/or AI solutions. The primary goals are: Create pipeline for our latest innovative solution, Acrobat Studio ( video ) Grow our business with both existing and new enterprise customers Establish new strategic relationships in assigned key accounts across several verticals Partner with internal and external ecosystem to sustain Adobe’s momentum in the Document Development What you’ll do Generation and closing of opportunities for Acrobat Studio in Named accounts in France Implementing comprehensive account sales strategies Developing and maintaining positive relationships on C-level and functional levels Using internal resources and business partners to provide solutions to customers’ challenges Follow up on leads generated by Marketing, build & grow pipeline to forecast your business Collaborate effectively with Adobe Account Executives and internal partners to deliver joint value propositions for enterprise customers Demo Acrobat Studio based on identified use case What you'll need to succeed Proven Enterprise Sales expertise with 5+ years of experience selling SaaS solutions to large enterprise accounts, ideally within the AI or Digital Workplace space. title: Global Portfolio & Investment Planning Lead
description: The Opportunity Within the Global Planning organization, the Global Portfolio & Investment Planning Lead functions as the strategic architect and operational driver of Adobe’s marketing portfolio planning. This position will take a leading role in transforming how planning grows across functions and markets, steering the organization toward a unified, portfolio management model. What you'll Do Enterprise Portfolio Strategy & Tradeoff Direction Build and evolve the enterprise portfolio strategy framework that connects Adobe’s business priorities to marketing plans and investments. Establish structured decision and trade-off frameworks across audiences, markets, and initiatives to guide prioritization. Lead multi-market portfolio reviews to evaluate allocation efficiency, strategic alignment, and business impact. Global Planning Platform Transformation & Future-State Vision Act as a principal designer of the future-state global marketing planning model at Adobe. Collaborate with Product & Planning, Platform, and Systems teams to define and implement the portfolio management model within Adobe’s planning systems. This work will also play a key role in shaping the future of marketing within Adobe’s own products; informing long-term product vision while driving tangible benefits for customers. Ensure portfolio data and planning workflows support cross-market comparability, global visibility, and executive decision-making. Enterprise Investment Governance Operate and continuously improve global marketing investment governance and portfolio oversight. Partner closely with GMO and Finance to ensure marketing portfolio governance aligns with FP&A and budgeting cycles. Executive Partnership & Cross-Functional Influence Serve as a strategic partner to the Head of Global Planning, helping build the direction of Adobe’s global marketing planning and portfolio strategy. Drive alignment across planning leads, marketing leadership, and finance leadership team members. What you need to succeed 7–10+ years of experience in marketing strategy, portfolio management, strategy and operations, finance partnership, or business strategy roles. Strong financial expertise with the ability to convert business strategy into capital allocation frameworks. Executive communication skills with the ability to distill complex data into clear recommendations. MBA or equivalent experience preferred but not required. title: Business Professional Sales Specialist C&P
description: Empowering everyone to Create At Adobe, we empower people—from emerging creators to global enterprises—to bring ideas to life and deliver exceptional digital experiences. Our solutions enable creativity, efficiency, and collaboration across all digital surfaces. They help individuals and organisations communicate more effectively and work smarter in a world powered by intelligent technology. The Opportunity Business professionals and consumers across the world are increasingly looking for ways to be both creative and productive in their daily work. AI and conversational interfaces are transforming how people tell stories, analyse information, and synthesise insights across documents and workflows. Customers want intuitive tools that work across every device, are easy to adopt, and deliver immediate value. As creativity and productivity continue to converge, Adobe is uniquely positioned to lead this transformation. AI is reshaping how work gets done—and how value is created. The Creativity & Productivity (C&P) sales team brings this vision to life for our customers. We work with named Enterprise accounts to demonstrate how Adobe delivers differentiated value and competes effectively in a rapidly evolving productivity landscape. The Role As a Business Professional Sales Specialist , your mission is to drive growth within the Enterprise business professional segment across the Nordics, with a focus on Document Cloud solutions , including Acrobat and Acrobat Studio . These solutions power increasingly interconnected enterprise workflows—helping customers understand, analyse, and synthesise documents, and transform them into high‑quality, creative outputs using Adobe Express . This fusion of insight and creativity sits at the core of Adobe’s value proposition and defines the charter of the C&P team. What You’ll Do Own and drive the full sales cycle for Adobe Document Cloud solutions, including Acrobat and Acrobat Studio, within named enterprise accounts Develop and complete strategic account plans aligned to customer business priorities and digital transformation initiatives Build positive relationships with C‑level executives and senior functional collaborators Partner with internal teams and Adobe ecosystem partners to deliver customer‑centric solutions Convert marketing‑generated leads into qualified opportunities and maintain a healthy, predictable pipeline Collaborate closely with Adobe Account Executives and internal specialists to present integrated value propositions Deliver compelling demonstrations of Acrobat Studio and Document Cloud solutions dedicated to practical applications What You Need to Succeed 5+ years of enterprise sales experience, with a proven track record of selling SaaS solutions to large organisations—preferably within the AI, Digital Workplace, or productivity space Strong passion for Acrobat Studio and enthusiasm for selling newly launched, AI‑first solutions Ability to position yourself as a trusted advisor by clearly articulating business value in the customer’s language Demonstrated success working cross‑functionally with Sales, Marketing, Product, and Partner teams A proactive attitude with the resilience to succeed in a competitive environment Experience in prospecting, account planning, needs analysis, executive presentations, negotiations, and proposal development Excellent communication and relationship‑building skills, with the ability to engage effectively at all levels Fluency in English and preferably proficiency in at least one Nordic language Why Adobe At Adobe, you’ll do meaningful work with some of the most innovative people in the industry. We are committed to creating exceptional employee experiences where everyone is respected, valued, and empowered to contribute. If you’re excited about shaping the future of work, creativity, and AI, we’d love to hear from you. title: Talent Partner, Technical Recruitment
description: Adobe's Technical Talent Acquisition Partner owns end-to-end recruitment for specialized roles across Engineering, AI/ML, and Product. This role combines hands-on recruiting with strategic influence — shaping data-driven hiring strategies, elevating candidate experience, and advising senior engineering leadership on talent decisions. Key Responsibilities Own recruiting from intake through close for engineering leadership and hard-to-fill technical roles, ensuring speed and quality at every stage. Build and maintain diverse talent pipelines in high-demand areas such as AI/ML, Cloud Infrastructure, Security, and Backend Engineering. Serve as a trusted partner to engineering directors and VPs — delivering market intelligence on talent availability, competitor hiring activity, and compensation benchmarks. Apply AI-enabled sourcing tools to improve efficiency, track funnel metrics to strengthen hiring predictability, and maintain dashboards that give leaders awareness of pipeline health. Deliver a high-touch, inclusive experience at every touchpoint, reflecting our "Adobe for All" commitment. Qualifications & Requirements Track record of success in full-cycle technical recruiting, ideally in high-volume, fast-paced product or engineering organizations. Deep familiarity with technical talent markets — software engineering, AI/ML, product management — including where to find passive candidates and how to assess role fit. Demonstrated ability to advise and push back constructively with director and VP-level stakeholders on headcount planning, offer strategy, and ambiguous hiring scenarios. Proficiency with ATS platforms and sourcing tools, with a habit of using data to diagnose bottlenecks and drive process improvements. What Success Looks Like The ideal partner doesn't just fill roles — they set the standard. Success means building repeatable sourcing strategies that anticipate demand, running structured interview processes that reduce bias, navigating compensation negotiations with confidence, and earning a seat at the table as a go-to advisor on talent strategy. title: Manager, Inside Sales
description: Adobe is a place where exceptional people work – don’t just take our word for it, our employees have recently voted us and Adobe Japan has been ranked 13th, for Great Place to Work. We’re proud to offer market competitive employee benefits designed to suit you and your lifestyle. Some of the great benefits include health insurance, global days off, wellness fund, parental leave, access to our Employee Stock Purchase Program and programs designed to help you continue to build your career. To learn more, please visit https://benefits.adobe.com/jp We also proudly empower our employees to get involved with corporate social responsibility and offer a broad range of opportunities to enable you to make a bigger impact through philanthropy, employee, and community engagement. We know that people are the differentiator in our business, and that's why we want to meet you! The Art of Sales is changing The concept of selling has been around for decades, but businesses are digital now. The way sales are conducted must modernize too. In most companies, sales teams are somewhere on the journey below: (1) Sales scratches out their own leads by prospecting their networks, sending one-off or batch emails. (2) Marketing turns over long lists of leads, which are typically contacts in your target audience (3) Marketing has established a repeated process of generating MQL, SAL & SQL that yield some measurable returns. They sometime add lead scoring to this mix. On the other hand, we at Adobe - ABD are a Modern Sales Team of 500+ Digital sellers, supported by analytics and data science teams. We are integrated with Adobe.com digital marketing & engagement funnel and are developing capabilities on picking cues from the customers demographic (who) & behavioral (what) data that they leave on their journey with our business to predict next ‘sales action’ that will lead to revenue conversions (Future state). The Challenge: We are looking for Digital Sellers who would be responsible for a defined patch of Adobe’s SMB customers and the role is pivoted around upselling and cross-selling Adobe Cloud based Solutions. You would be responsible for the Creativity & Productivity portfolio of Adobe. The incumbent would be responsible for helping the customer adopt the solution with ease, optimizing their product experience and guiding them to internal support teams if need be. What you’ll do • Develop understanding of Adobe's Creativity & Productivity line of products and lead with value-led conversations with customers for these solutions. • Create a value-based relationship with new & existing Adobe SMB customers. • Drive Up-sell & Cross-sell by prioritizing accounts with highest propensity to buy by clearly defining ideal customer profile and contact them via phones & emails. • Execute Marketing Qualified Leads with a defined SLA to maximize Revenue. • Research customer contracts and purchasing history in Adobe's various customer management systems & external sources such as LinkedIn, ZoomInfo, etc. to figure out the expansion opportunity in an account • Managing the opportunity pipeline from week to week, providing accurate and timely updates to management on progress and outlook. • Collaborate with Solution Specialists to maximize footprint of growing Adobe Solution streams like Adobe Sign, Substance, Stock, Frame.io, etc. • Engage as required, with the supporting functional teams to resolve issues raised by customers related to their Creative Cloud Subscriptions. What you need to succeed •Fluent in Japanese language and Business level English speaking/writing ability •SMB Segment exposure and proven ability to manage a large customer set • 3 -5 Years’ experience in a similar role, with experience in selling SAAS solutions preferred • Excellent communication Skills, both oral and written • Flair for Technology and ability to lead a customer conversation with value-selling rather than product features pitch. • Demonstrated ability to be a quick learner. Task oriented with focus and drive to complete tasks at hand. • Strong organization, follow-through and documentation skills suitable for customer communication . • People management experience Adobe for All Adobe strives to create an environment where our employees can do their best work and drive their career growth and development based on their personal goals. title: Sales Support Rep Analyst
description: The Opportunity Join Adobe's dynamic Worldwide Sales Operations team as a Sales Support Rep Analyst, where you will be at the heart of our global sales execution. This role offers a distinctive opportunity to drive operational excellence, automation, and CRM optimization, making a significant impact on our sales processes. Based in Noida, Uttar Pradesh, India, this hybrid position provides the perfect balance between office collaboration and remote flexibility. What you'll Do Offer outstanding support to our Global Sales Organization, addressing process, system, and access challenges within Salesforce, Dynamics 365, and other critical sales applications. Manage support tickets through ServiceNow and CRM workflows, ensuring strict adherence to SLAs and maintaining high-resolution quality benchmarks. Collaborate with Product Owners and Automation COE teams for customer concerns and recurring issue analysis. Support configuration and troubleshooting in Salesforce and Dynamics 365, validating CRM workflows, business rules, and integration flows. title: Software Development Engineer - Adobe Sites Optimizer
description: The Opportunity Are you excited to work on a 0-1 product that helps businesses elevate their digital presence for maximum revenue? Join our team as a Software Development Engineer and help create innovative AEM Sites Optimizer and LLM Optimizer that transform how digital-first companies approach improvements. It is a fast-paced setting where your knowledge of AI/ML Optimizations will impact customer success directly. Our team builds AI-first solutions that lets marketing teams quickly measure, experiment and activate optimizations that drive high-performing impact for the current transformation of the digital landscape. What will you do As a Software Engineer, you will directly contribute to core features of the product by building cloud services that integrate AI for solutions that drive high impact on our customers. Discover success metrics for customers by analyzing meaningful data from various sources. Be in charge of comprehensive technical audits and improve strategies to optimize the experiences of our customers taking into consideration GEO, SEO, accessibility crawl-ability, index-ability and demonstrating insights for future automation. Turn your findings into solid hypotheses that will be meaningful at wider customer scale. Automate your hypotheses into self-learning AI-first software with goal to improve conversion, website/off-site performance, GEO and user experience. Manipulate and build services that integrate traditional ML and generative AI to extract insights from large data sets. Design and implement prompt engineering strategies to optimize model performance. Utilize Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and related techniques to enhance model output. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to identify and address AI-related challenges. Demonstrate the latest innovations in generative AI to stakeholders. What you need to succeed Excellent problem-solving skills, strong communication, and a proactive, collaborative approach, growing in fast-paced environments. Technical Skills: Advanced degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Machine Learning, or a related field. Experience with JavaScript or Python and proficiency with modern frameworks. Proven expertise in crafting and developing scalable and resilient applications using concepts like CDNs, Cache, Server Side Rendering, Lambda functions, Containers, Queues, Workers, APIs, all in Cloud Platforms(Azure, AWS) Experience in working with SQL & NoSQL Databases Experience in integrating ML/Generative AI models. Knowledge in both building but also operating a service: CI/CD deployments, monitoring and alerting, Kubernetes-based deployments Pluses: Experience in machine learning, data science or experience working with Generative AI models. Proficiency in ML frameworks like PyTorch and TensorFlow. Proven experience with Large Language Models (e.g. GPT, BERT) and prompt engineering. Ability to quickly generate working code with a Generative AI coding copilot. Expertise in fine-tuning and deploying AI models in production environments. Strong knowledge of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and related techniques. Familiarity with marketing performance metrics and their application in AI models. Ability to suggest new projects and make a case for their benefits to our business. Experience & Education: Bachelor’s or equivalent experience in CS or related field 5+ years of proven experience working with the above mentioned tech stack Portfolio of work (GitHub, live projects) Adobe is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity and affirmative action employer. title: Scaled Program Lead
description: The Opportunity Adobe’s Unified Platform is operating at significant scale: hundreds of concurrent programs, thousands of interdependent work items, and globally distributed product, program, user experience, and technical groups. Individual Program Managers focus on execution within their portfolios. Meanwhile, the organization requires a system-wide program oversight capability to identify risks earlier, improve predictability, and evolve delivery continuously. This high-leverage role of Scaled Program Management Lead was developed to meet that need. It builds on the core expectations of a Lead Program Manager—strong execution, cross‑functional leadership, and delivery accountability—while extending them across the platform. Rather than owning a bounded portfolio, this role establishes the mechanisms, data, and operating model required to manage world-class delivery at scale. This is an individual contributor role for a highly experienced, “six-sigma-type” program leader. The candidate must understand how software is built and delivered. They will collaborate closely with development teams. They will then transform those insights into scalable systems and approaches for the wider organization. At the scale of our platform, small improvements in focus, predictability, and flow translate into outsized impact. This role exists to institutionalize those improvements —to make data‑driven, scalable delivery part of how the platform operates every day, not a one‑off effort. This approach ensures that how we work evolves as fast as what we build. What You’ll Do Platform‑Scale Program Leadership Operate horizontally to provide system‑level insight into delivery health, risks, dependencies, and bottlenecks across hundreds of active programs and development teams . Establish and run scalable program management mechanisms that complement (not replace) portfolio Program Managers and engineering leadership. Act as a trusted partner to platform and executive leadership by framing delivery trade‑ offs, systemic risks, and opportunities using data‑backed insights. Data‑Driven Risk & Opportunity Management Leverage JIRA, GitHub, and related delivery systems to surface leading indicators of execution risk, capacity imbalance, and dependency friction. Apply analytical thinking and AI‑assisted techniques to move from reactive status reporting to predictive insights (e.g., cycle time trends, backlog health, delivery confidence). Identify recurring structural issues and translate them into actionable interventions, tooling improvements, or operating model changes. Scaled Systems & Ways of Working Invent and institutionalize scalable mechanisms that improve clarity, focus, and flow across teams without adding unnecessary process overhead. Partner with existing Program Managers to standardize where it helps, while preserving flexibility for different product and engineering contexts. Continuously evaluate and evolve program management practices based on real‑world outcomes and data, not theory. Grounded Execution Perspective Stay closely connected to on‑the‑ground execution alongside engineering groups to ensure insights and mechanisms reflect real delivery realities. Translate hands‑on delivery experience into platform‑level patterns and recommendations. Communicate complex technical and programmatic concepts clearly to both technical and non‑technical partners. What You Bring 8+ years of experience in software engineering, technical program management, or a closely related role, with direct exposure to large‑scale, multi‑team delivery. Demonstrated experience operating across portfolios or platforms, identifying systemic delivery issues rather than isolated program risks. Strong analytical orientation, with experience using data to drive decisions around prioritization, capacity, flow, and predictability. Hands‑on experience with delivery tooling such as JIRA and GitHub, and comfort working with data pipelines, dashboards, and AI‑assisted analysis. Deep understanding of what it takes to deliver software—from planning through execution and release—and credibility with partners. Proven ability to influence without authority, drive alignment, and establish durable operating mechanisms in complex organizations. title: Director, Creative Strategy & AI Innovation
description: Our Company Changing the world through digital experiences is what Adobe’s all about. We give everyone—from emerging artists to global brands, everything they need to design and deliver exceptional digital experiences! We’re passionate about empowering people to create beautiful and powerful images, videos, and apps, and transform how companies interact with customers across every screen. We’re on a mission to hire the very best and are committed to creating exceptional employee experiences where everyone is respected and has access to equal opportunity. We realize that new ideas can come from everywhere in the organization, and we know the next big idea could be yours. The Opportunity Join Studio, Adobe’s in-house creative agency, where creativity, culture, and business strategy converge. Studio delivers high-visibility, global work across brand platforms, flagship campaigns, keynotes, product launches, films, and culturally resonant digital experiences. As Director, Creative Strategy & AI Innovation , you will lead the strategic engine of Studio connecting creativity, product, audience, and emerging technology. Operating at the intersection of Product, Marketing, and Innovation, you will ensure that the work is not only creatively compelling, but deeply aligned to customer needs, product truth, and Adobe’s broader AI vision, translating product innovation into stories that people understand, value, and adopt. This role goes beyond traditional creative strategy. You will define how insight, technology, and creativity come together, shaping platform ideas that are grounded in real audience understanding and unlocked by new capabilities in AI. You will help the organization move from making campaigns to building enduring, intelligent creative systems that evolve with the product and the customer. You bring a deep understanding of modern audiences, especially creators and the communities they build and how culture moves through participation, not just messaging. You will ensure our work is not only seen, but adopted, remixed, and shared, designing platforms that creators want to engage with and build upon. You will work in close partnership with the VP of Creative to shape the strategic direction of Studio, ensuring ideas are aligned to creative ambition, product innovation, and long-term brand impact. You will partner closely with Creative, Production, Product, and Marketing leadership to translate complex inputs from product capabilities to audience insight into clear, compelling strategic direction and product narratives. This role defines the strategic direction of the work, partnering closely with Production & Program leadership on execution and with Content Operations on systems and workflows. What You’ll Do Lead the development of creative and communications strategy across high-impact brand platforms, campaigns, and product storytelling ensuring product innovation is translated into clear, compelling, and culturally relevant narratives Act as the connective tissue between Product, Marketing, and Creative, ensuring all work is grounded in customer insight, product truth, and business priorities Define and articulate clear strategic narratives that inform and inspire creative direction, from initial brief through execution Ensure all work ladders back to a deep understanding of audience needs, behaviors, and cultural context — with a particular focus on creators and their communities Define how Adobe shows up for creators identifying opportunities to build platforms, tools, and narratives that enable participation, co-creation, and community growth Shape work that is designed for engagement, not just exposure creating ideas that invite interaction, remixing, and cultural momentum. Develop platform ideas that are designed to scale across brand, product, and performance channels, creating consistency and long-term value Partner with Product and Innovation teams to align creative strategy with Adobe’s AI roadmap and emerging capabilities Identify opportunities where AI can unlock new forms of creativity, storytelling, personalization, and content generation Define how AI shapes not only execution, but the strategic approach to creativity—informing insight, ideation, and adaptive content systems Translate complex technologies and product capabilities into clear, compelling, human-centered stories that drive understanding, relevance, and adoption Define how Adobe’s products show up in the work — shaping narratives tha

title: Director of Product Management, Media Generation
description: The Opportunity We are seeking a Director of Product Management to lead the Media Generation product group. You will own the product vision, strategy, and roadmap for Media Generation and serve as the product leader across a matrixed organization spanning engineering, AI research, design, user research, product marketing, and go to market teams. You’ll work closely with our internal team and external partners, the leading Generative AI companies to shape joint product experiences that lead the market and work quickly to implement the latest models in short timeframes to ensure Adobe’s customers have the best models available. This role is a rare opportunity to shape a new, AI first product at global scale. You will help define the next generation of creative tooling, work closely with customers, and help Studio become an essential creative surface for the next generation of artists, designers, and creative technologists! What You’ll Do · Define and own the long-term product vision, articulating how creators concept, generate, iterate, and produce their work · Lead the Media Generation product roadmap by identifying high impact opportunities, prioritizing features, defining success metrics, and supporting end to end delivery. · Build creator first workflows that feel powerful, expressive, and intuitive for artists, designers, and technical creatives. · Partner closely with engineering, AI research, design, user research, and platform teams to deliver a cohesive, scalable product experience. · Manage fast moving, agentic product development efforts with cross functional teams · Manage and implement fast moving features at scale in close collaboration with the industry’s leading Generative AI partners so Adobe always has the best model available to our customers. · Translate complex AI systems and abstractions into clear, usable product experiences that work across a wide range of creator skill levels. · Drive rapid iteration through prototyping, experimentation, and direct engagement with creators. · Help how Media Generation fits into Adobe’s broader AI platform strategy and Creative Cloud ecosystem. · Share the product vision inside and outside Adobe, gathering feedback from customers, partners, and internal teams to guide product direction. · Build, lead, and mentor a high performing team of product managers by setting a high bar for product craft, ownership, and execution. What’s Needed for Success · 10+ years of product management experience, including leadership of complex, multi surface products. · A deep understanding of generative and assistive ai technologies · Proven experience building products for creators, artists, designers, or creative technologists. · Experience leading product in a matrixed organization and influencing across engineering, design, research, and go to market teams. · Demonstrated ability to take ambiguous, zero to one ideas and turn them into shipped, scaled products. · Demonstrated ability to lead teams implementing new partner technology at fast turns in a dynamic market environment · Strong product judgment and systems thinking, with comfort working at the intersection of UX, technical architecture, and AI capabilities. · A builder’s mindset, comfortable with ambiguity, fast iteration, and learning through shipping. · Strong communication skills and ability to align senior leaders and cross functional partners around a clear product narrative. · Experience with AI powered products, developer platforms, or visual or graph-based tools is a strong plus. · Bachelor’s degree required with a preference for Computer Science or Product Design. title: Solutions Consultant, Creativity and Productivity - Education
description: About the role At Adobe, we’re changing the world through digital experiences. Within Education, our mission is to empower institutions, educators, and students to create, communicate, and learn more effectively using Adobe technologies. The Solutions Consultant, Education plays a meaningful role across the entire sales lifecycle. They support pre-sales discovery and develop solutions, then assist with the transition into Customer Success. Their work helps bring value and close large Adobe Creative Cloud and Adobe Document Cloud opportunities. This is a highly customer‑facing, hands‑on role suited for an individual who is curious, proactive, and driven to move the work forward. You will work closely with Sales, Customer Success, Marketing, and Product teams to support higher education customers globally. Travel requirement: Up to 50% travel is required to support customer engagements and industry events. What you’ll do Support the full sales cycle, partnering with Account Executives through solution positioning, demonstrations, and deal closure. Act as a trusted advisor to higher education institutions, helping them reimagine creative, teaching, learning, and document workflows using Adobe solutions. Deliver compelling, hands‑on demonstrations of Adobe Creative Cloud and Document Cloud, tailored to academic, IT, and executive decision makers. Provide sales teams with technical pre-sales assistance and direction on business and technical requirements. Collaborate cross‑functionally with Sales, Customer Success, Marketing, and Product partners to ensure continuity from pre‑sales through post‑sales adoption. Translate customer needs into scalable solution architectures and recommended workflows across creative and document use cases. Participate in customer workshops, executive briefings, roadshows, Adobe events and industry conferences to drive thought leadership in higher education. What You Need To Succeed Deep understanding of Adobe Creative Cloud and Adobe Document Cloud, including creative, productivity, and document‑centric workflows. Experience in Generative AI and AI-powered workflows, demonstrated through one or both of the following areas: timely engineering and Generative AI or automated document and research workflows. Proven experience in a Solutions Consulting, Technical Pre‑Sales, Sales Engineering, or Consulting role. Demonstrated ability to support cross-functional collaborators across Sales and Customer Success in a highly matrixed environment. Strong customer‑facing skills with the ability to communicate confidently with academic leaders, IT leaders, and high-level decision makers. High levels of initiative and ownership: someone who is genuinely motivated to roll up their sleeves and help get the work done! Skills we’d like to see (although not critical for success) Teaching or instructional experience, preferably within Higher Education. Familiarity with higher education pedagogy, and institutional challenges. What success looks like You enable education customers to clearly understand the value of Adobe Creative Cloud and Document Cloud across teaching, learning, and institutional productivity. You help Sales close meaningful, value‑based deals while setting Customer Success up for strong post‑sale adoption. You are seen internally as a collaborative, reliable partner who brings energy, curiosity, and momentum to complex initiatives. title: Senior Director Legal Counsel Creative Cloud
description: Our Company Changing the world through digital experiences is what Adobe's all about. We give everyone, from emerging artists to global brands, everything they need to design and deliver exceptional digital experiences. We're passionate about empowering everyone to craft beautiful and powerful images, videos, and apps, and transform how companies interact with customers across every screen. We're on a mission to hire the very best and are committed to building exceptional employee experiences where everyone is respected and has access to equal opportunity. We realize that new ideas can come from everywhere in the organization, and we know the next big idea could be yours! The Opportunity Adobe is seeking a Senior Director, Legal to serve as the lead attorney for the Creative Cloud (CC) business unit on a global scale. Creative Cloud encompasses over 20 desktop and mobile apps, including Photoshop, Lightroom, Illustrator, Premiere Pro, and After Effects — along with integrated services such as Adobe Fonts, and cloud storage. This is a high-impact leadership role at the intersection of law and business strategy. The right candidate is, first and foremost, a business affairs attorney — someone who leads with a deep understanding of business objectives and combines that with expert legal judgment to help the business achieve its goals while right-sizing risk. This means developing a thorough command of CC's product lines, customer segments, go-to-market strategies, platforms, and competitive position, and translating that knowledge into clear, practical legal guidance. This role reports to the VP and Deputy General Counsel of Creativity & Productivity (C&P), who reports to the Chief Legal Officer, and works closely with colleagues across the Legal & Government Relations (LGR) organization and throughout Adobe. What You'll Do Team Leadership & Development Lead, grow, and inspire a team of highly talented attorneys and legal specialists supporting CC product compliance globally. Empower team members to make sound, independent risk decisions grounded in a clear understanding of laws, regulations, business objectives, and strategic priorities. Maintain a high-quality, well-staffed team by monitoring workload, anticipating headcount needs, advocating for resources, and shifting support to align with evolving business unit priorities. Foster a team culture of business partnership and collaboration— ensuring attorneys work collaboratively with business teams rather than acting as gatekeepers. Legal Strategy & Risk Advising Serve as the primary legal advisor to CC business and product leadership, advising senior executives on legal risks across all CC product and business activities worldwide. Develop and drive risk and innovation strategy across a broad and rapidly evolving international legal landscape, including consumer protection, IP, antitrust, licensing, and applicable international laws. Connect the dots across business strategy, product plans, and legal risk — breaking down silos, ensuring consistency, and eliminating blind spots across the organization. Anticipate legal and market trends, look around corners, and put plans in place to address emerging risks before they materialize. Cross-Functional Partnership Partner closely with privacy, trust and safety, trade compliance, Employment, Corporate, Product, Security, and Go-To-Market teams to manage CC product compliance end-to-end. Develop deeply meaningful relationships with senior stakeholders across Adobe, building the organizational knowledge and trust needed to get things done effectively. Develop a deep understanding of CC customer segments — including Education, Government, Enterprise, Teams, and Individual — the offers provided to each, associated terms, and routes to market. Support CC’s back-end platforms where legal compliance requirements are essential to Adobe's overall compliance profile.
