title: Manager Inside Sales
description: Role: Manager, Inside Sales Location: Remote India Shift: North America (6:30pm 3:30am IST) Role Summary Ensures that the customer is satisfied with company projects and services. Works closely with Sales Account Manager and oversees day-to-day relationship with client. Ensures that schedules and budgets are met according to contractual agreements. Works with client to cultivate future projects and qualify new opportunities. Consults with other team members (project management/services/support/customer care) to be sure mutual objectives are met. Job Description Summary Administers/designs the inside/telesales representatives’ sales incentive plan. Trains, mentors, coaches, and supervises inside/telesales staff. Has thorough knowledge of the organization’s products/services. Prioritizes and allocates resources. Typically works with the field sales organization to ensure that representatives are provided adequate support in the field. May work with inside/telesales representatives to develop channel partnerships. Responsible for market development, setting targets, and ensuring that revenue is generated. Adobe Changing the world through digital experiences is what Adobe’s all about. We give everyone—from emerging artists to global brands—everything they need to design and deliver exceptional digital experiences. We’re passionate about empowering people to build alluring and powerful images, videos, and apps, and transform how companies interact with customers across every screen. We’re on a mission to hire the very best and are committed to creating exceptional employee experiences. We realize that new ideas can come from everywhere in the organization, and we know the next big idea could be yours. The Art of Sales is changing The concept of selling has been around for decades, but businesses are digital now. The way sales are conducted must modernise too. In most companies, sales teams are somewhere on the journey below: Sales scratches out their own leads by prospecting their networks, sending one-off or batch emails. Marketing turns over long lists of leads, which are typically contacts in your target audience Marketing has established a repeated process of generating MQL, SAL & SQL that yield some measurable returns. They sometime add lead scoring to this mix. On the other hand, we at Adobe - GBD are a Modern Sales team of 250+ Digital sellers, supported by analytics and data science teams. We are integrated with Adobe.com digital marketing & engagement funnel and are developing capabilities on picking cues from the customers demographic (who) & behavioral (what) data that they leave on their journey with our business to predict next ‘sales action’ that will lead to revenue conversions (Future state). The Challenge: We are looking for a Sales Manager and Leader who is responsible for leading sales for a team and enable the Digital Sales team to continue exceeding the defined sales goals and KPIs across the North Americas territory. You would be responsible for the Digital Media portfolio of Adobe. An ideal candidate would be an inspiring leader with a consistent record of leading successful sales operations on field or in Digital Sales of reasonable size and possess the ability to turn findings into executable plans. He / She must also be an inspiring leader able to build, develop & inspire a successful team. What you will do: As a Leader: Leading the team by coaching, mentoring & motivating the team with a high level of EQ & becoming a trusted leader for the team. Should possess strong collaboration skills, as the Digital Sellers leverage other teams for their own success. Driving the highest level of performance from the team at the same time role modelling & instilling Adobe values in the team. Building knowledge of Adobe products and services – their features, benefits, prices, and additional services. Developing ways to coach the team to identify customer needs and pitch the relevant product benefits to complete a sale. Working with Marketing, Sales Operation, Business units, Corporate Retention teams to source sales or retention campaigns, marketing leads and execute. Analyze business goals, customer & agent data & daily reports to find areas of continuous improvement. As Sales Professional: The Adobe Manager will manage a set of sellers and will be accountable to help identify, drive & close revenue from existing Adobe Direct customers . title: Senior Director, Product Management
description: The Opportunity Adobe is redefining creativity, document intelligence, and digital experience through AI. We're looking for an AI-native Senior Director of Product Management to lead the Developer Platform and AI Platform organization - the foundational layer that powers almost every capability Adobe ships. This role sits at the intersection of platform engineering, applied AI, and developer experience, and is responsible for the strategy, roadmap, and execution of the systems that make Adobe an AI-first company. You won't just manage a product portfolio. You'll architect the internal and external platforms used by over ten thousand engineers, hundreds of product teams, and millions of third-party developers. You'll own the developer experience end-to-end - from AI coding tools to, API design and SDK strategy, to workload hosting and gateways, to the plugin and extension ecosystem that lets the world build on top of Adobe’s technology. This is a role for someone who has shipped AI-powered platforms at scale, thinks in systems rather than features, and believes that the right platform is one developers never want to leave. What You'll Own Developer Platform & Ecosystem. Own the developer platform product portfolio – from the multi-cloud Kubernetes platform to API gateways, SDKs, developer tools, source code and artifact repositories, and the customer-facing application builder experience and the marketplace of extensions and plugins. Set the north-star for Adobe's external developer experience and drive adoption metrics. You'll ensure that third-party developers can build rich, performant integrations with Adobe products using modern toolchains and AI-augmented workflows. Define the vision for infrastructure that turns Adobe from a product suite into a programmable platform. Platform Strategy. Own the overall strategic vision for Adobe’s Developer Platform. This group serves both internal Adobe developers – helping them “create better software, faster” – as well as external developers at customer and partner companies, who want to leverage our technologies, integrate and extend them. Execution. Build and maintain a culture of relentless focus on execution. Remove process bottlenecks, streamline communications, automate the SDLC intelligently with AI, ensuring that the right moments are created to engage the humans in the loop and ensure that judgement and taste are being applied at the critical process junctures. AI Tooling for Internal Engineering. Lead the product vision for internal AI-powered developer tools - code generation assistants, automated testing and review, intelligent CI/CD, knowledge retrieval systems, and agentic workflow tooling. You'll partner with Engineering, IT, and Security to raise the productivity floor for every engineer at Adobe while maintaining the security and compliance posture the enterprise demands. Communication. Maintain regular cadence of communication with client leaders and engineering teams to keep them informed about our plans, priorities and progress, and to hear their feedback. Act as the Chief Marketing Officer for the DP team, with support and help from the entire Engineering and Program Leadership team. Evangelism. Serve as a voice of the platform across the organization and the broader external technology community - highlighting capabilities and use-cases to client teams and actively selling our wares to encourage adoption. Team Leadership. Build, mentor, and lead a world-class product management organization of 15–25 PMs across multiple sub-teams. Set the hiring bar, develop career frameworks, and cultivate a culture where rigorous technical thinking coexists with deep customer empathy. Act as a leader of the broader organization, creating an environment that builds trust and strong working relationships between your Product Managers and their Engineering, Design and Program Management partners. What We're Looking For Platform thinking at enterprise scale. You've built or led platforms consumed by large internal engineering organizations and/or external developer ecosystems. You understand API design principles, extensibility patterns, multi-tenancy, rate limiting, versioning strategy, and what it takes to earn and keep developer trust. Experience with developer relations and ecosystem growth is strongly valued. AI-native product leadership. 12+ years of product management experience, with at least 3 years leading an AI/ML platform and/or developer platform offering. You

title: Director of Design, Frame.io
description: The opportunity Frame.io, an Adobe Company, is changing the future of how creative work gets done by helping over 1 million creative professionals seamlessly collaborate from all over the world. We have built a product creatives recognize and love through constant innovation and incredible attention to detail. We're looking for a Director of Design to lead a core part of our product design organization and drive both vision and execution for how creative professionals collaborate at a pivotal moment. Creative workflows and tools are rapidly evolving with the advent of generative AI and new technologies like Frame.io’s Camera to Cloud. In this role, you'll lead and inspire a team of talented design managers and individual contributors, setting the creative bar for our product experiences while partnering closely with leadership across product, engineering, and research. This is a role for an accomplished design leader who combines exceptional strategic thinking with a genuine passion for craft. You'll be equally comfortable defining multi-year product vision, developing organizational capability, and regularly rolling up your sleeves to provide hands-on creative direction. You should possess deep expertise in product design, proven ability to build and scale high-performing teams, and an unwavering commitment to design excellence. Your leadership will shape the future of Frame.io, pioneering new experiences that shape how millions of creatives work in the next era of creativity. What you'll do Build, mentor, and grow a world-class design team. You'll attract exceptional talent, develop design managers and senior individual contributors, and create a culture and process where designers thrive and do their best work. Shape strategic direction across Frame.io and Adobe. You'll partner with executive stakeholders to translate business objectives into compelling design vision, identify emerging opportunities in creative collaboration, and ensure our product roadmap reflects deep user understanding and market insight. Provide creative direction and uphold design excellence. You’ll set and maintain the highest standards for design quality. You'll provide hands-on creative direction on key initiatives, conduct design reviews that elevate the work, and ensure every product experience reflects Frame.io's commitment to extraordinary craft and attention to detail. Partner closely with product, engineering, research, and marketing leadership. You’ll work cross-functionally to ensure seamless collaboration and aligned execution. You'll advocate for design thinking throughout the organization, influence strategic decisions, and build strong relationships that unlock the team's full potential. Ensure deep customer empathy drives every design decision. You'll establish frameworks for integrating user research throughout the design process, personally engage with customers to understand their needs, and inspire your team to build products that truly serve creative professionals. Stay on the bleeding edge of creative technology. The creative collaboration landscape is rapidly evolving with generative AI, new production tools, and shifting creative processes. You'll immerse yourself in these innovations, identify opportunities to shape Frame.io and Adobe’s direction, and provide visionary leadership that keeps us at the forefront of our category. Serve as the champion and storyteller for your team's work. You'll represent design in executive forums, craft compelling narratives that bring design decisions to life, and build consensus across leadership. Through excellent presentations, clear communication, and strong relationships, you'll ensure your team's contributions are understood and valued, translating design work into business impact. What you need to succeed 5+ years of design leadership, experience managing managers and leading design organizations, plus 10+ years of hands-on product design experience. You've successfully built and scaled design teams at companies that ship high-quality digital products. Exceptional creative direction capabilities. You have an outstanding aesthetic sense and ability to provide clear, actionable creative direction. You have impeccable taste, know what great looks like, and can articulate a compelling vision that inspires teams to push beyond good to exceptional. A desire to stay in the work. title: Professional Services Consultant
description: The Opportunity This Professional Services Consultant role offers an outstanding opportunity to work closely with enterprise clients across enterprises in domains such as banking, telco, airlines, and hospitality, understanding their technical and business requirements to implement best-in-class MarTech solutions — including Adobe Experience Cloud and adjacent platforms — that deliver measurable value. Your key objective is to ensure successful delivery across customer data platform (CDP), journey orchestration, campaign management and / or content management solutions, while maintaining a high level of customer satisfaction. You will leverage AI-powered tools and emerging automation capabilities to accelerate project delivery, and you are expected to pick up new platforms and methodologies quickly as the MarTech landscape evolves. What you’ll do Act as a domain expert in enterprise MarTech platforms — with strong focus on CDPs, journey orchestration, campaign / content management, and data ingestion pipelines — such as Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Experience Manager, Adobe Journey Optimizer, and Adobe Campaign. Translate marketing domain knowledge — from sectors such as banking, telco, airlines, and hospitality — into actionable data models, segmentation and content strategies, and personalization use cases. Navigate complex project tasks, proactively addressing challenges including clarifying business requirements, optimizing performance, and integrating third-party products. Deliver consulting services for enterprise MarTech implementations, assisting clients and partners with architecture design, delivery execution, issue management, end-to-end testing, and stakeholder engagement. Facilitate customer discovery sessions, guiding clients in defining their business requirements and key performance indicators to help them derive the full value of their Adobe solutions. Apply AI tools (e.g. co-pilots, generative assistants, LLM-powered workflows) to accelerate solution design, documentation, and client deliverables. What you need to succeed: 5+ years' experience in IT consulting or implementation for enterprise SaaS or MarTech solutions — such as CDPs, marketing automation, content management systems, or data ingestion platforms — with expertise in requirements definition, architecture, design, and delivery. Marketing domain knowledge in at least one industry vertical: banking / financial services, telecommunications, airlines, hospitality, or retail. Demonstrated ability to connect business marketing goals to technical implementation. Demonstrated ability to learn new platforms and tools rapidly — including AI-powered development and productivity tools — and integrate them into day-to-day consulting work. Familiarity with enterprise MarTech, including CDPs (e.g. Adobe Experience Platform, Segment, Treasure Data, Tealium), marketing automation (e.g. Adobe Journey Optimizer, Adobe Campaign, Salesforce Marketing Cloud, Braze), or content management platforms (e.g. AEM, Contentful, Sitecore). An approach centered on delivering outcomes, responsiveness, a high degree of integrity, and a strong dedication to customer success. Excellent creative and analytical problem-solving skills. Good interpersonal, presentation, and communication skills — written and verbal. Genuine interest in emerging technologies and methodologies with a passion for staying ahead of trends in digital marketing technologies. Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience. Travel up to 50% of the time. Bonus Experience: Experience with data ingestion pipelines, event streaming (e.g. Kafka, Kinesis), or ETL/ELT tools used in large-scale marketing data environments. Hands-on experience with tag management and data collection layers (e.g. Launch, Tealium iQ, Google Tag Manager). Practical use of AI coding assistants, prompt engineering, or LLM-driven workflow automation in a professional context. Hands-on experience writing / debugging in one or more scripting or programming languages such as JavaScript or TypeScript, Java or Python – for use cases including data transformation, data querying, API integrations or workflow automation. title: Senior Product Engineer  — Android
description: The Opportunity Our Android mobile team is part of the Adobe Video organization, where we build video creation and editing experiences for storytellers across desktop, mobile, and web. We move fast, care deeply about craft, and believe the best products come from engineers who think like product owners. Are you a Senior Android Engineer who leads with judgment across code, product, and design? Do you push back on specs that aren't right — and help define better ones? If you ship high-quality Android experiences, know how to leverage AI tools to accelerate your work, and bring genuine taste and instinct to every feature you own, we'd love to meet you! What You'll Do We work end-to-end, and so will you. In this role, you'll own complex Android features from interaction design through engineering and production. You’ll partner with Product, Design, and Engineering leaders to shape and refine product requirements using your technical and UX instincts — bringing a point of view, not just implementation. You'll use AI-assisted and agentic coding tools to accelerate delivery, applying strong judgment to direct AI sessions, evaluate output critically, and refactor where needed. We expect you to make confident scope and priority calls that balance craft, velocity, and user impact — and to mentor peers while maintaining high standards across the codebase. We ship, learn, and improve: you'll test assumptions early, iterate on real feedback, and treat every release as an opportunity to raise the bar on usability, performance, and creative outcomes. What We're Looking For You'll need: 10+ years shipping Android applications Deep proficiency in Android Java and/or Kotlin. Strong expertise in multi-threading, memory management, and performance profiling Product instinct and UX sensibility — you know the Google’s Material Design guidelines deeply, notice when an interaction feels off, and can articulate why it matters Comfort with ambiguity; you're energized by finding the right answer, not just implementing a given one Proficiency with AI-assisted development tools — including agentic coding workflows — with the ability to direct and orchestrate AI sessions effectively and critically evaluate and validate generated output Clear, precise communication — written and verbal — across technical and product contexts A deep understanding of mobile video editing workflows — or the drive to develop it fast Even Better If You Have Experience with Jetpack Compose, Media3 and video/multimedia development or a track record of influencing product or design decisions rather than just implementing them. Familiarity with design systems or prototyping tools like Figma — enough for a real dialogue with designers — is a plus, as is experience building creative or video production tools or using video editing apps. Why Adobe Video? We offer a small-team environment with big ownership — you'll work at the intersection of world-class creative software and AI-augmented engineering. We invest in our people with industry-leading benefits, including comprehensive health coverage, generous equity, flexible time off, and access to Adobe's full suite of creative tools. We believe diverse perspectives make our products better, and we're committed to building a team where everyone can do the best work of their careers. Are you ready to shape tools used by millions of creators worldwide? We'd love to hear from you! title: Strategy and Operations Lead
description: The Opportunity Join Adobe as Strategy and Operations Lead - Adobe on Adobe, where you will play a pivotal role in driving business performance, program rigor, and effective communications. This outstanding opportunity allows you to collaborate with finance teams and lead annual planning and budgeting cycles. Become an integral part of a world-class group driving value realization for Adobe teams using our own technology, and helping to create a strong operational foundation that allows our consultants to deliver flawless and remarkable results! What you'll Do Manage the annual planning and quarterly budget evaluation cycles for the Adobe on Adobe consulting organization. Collaborate with finance on forecasting, budgeting, and headcount planning. Review and consolidate travel and expense requests, tracking against forecasted spend. Work with sr. leadership to manage budgets and forecasts. Coordinate with HR Ops and partner liaisons to source and onboard partner resources. Lead content creation for quarterly all-hands meetings and maintain the Adobe on Adobe program charter. Advise Global Delivery Center operations team on staffing and opportunities to build more alignment between US and India-based consultants. What you need to succeed 8+ years in strategy, business operations, consulting, or services operations. Strong financial modeling and planning experience. Experience supporting delivery organizations or large, matrixed teams. Ability to influence without formal authority in a matrixed environment. Clear, confident communicator with executive presence. Highly collaborative, able to build trust quickly across functions. Comfortable working in ambiguity and providing a point of view where there are gaps. Preferred qualifications: Familiarity with Adobe’s products is a strong plus. title: Manager, Forward Deployed Creative Technologist
description: The Opportunity Every global brand is now a content factory. They need thousands of on-brand asset variations every quarter — static, video, and increasingly 3D — and they need to ship them in days, not weeks. The customer who figures this out first wins the next decade of marketing. We are the team that gets them there. Forward Deployed Creative Technologists (FDCTs) embed with enterprise customers, learn their content supply chain end-to-end, and craft custom AI-powered creative workflows on top of Adobe Firefly Services and Firefly Creative Production (our node-based workflow builder). We are not pre-sales. We are not consultants. We are technical creators who ship . You will lead a team of FDCTs as a hands-on player-coach . ~50% of your week is in code, in customer rooms, in demos — leading by example, not by directive. The other ~50% is hiring, growing, coaching, and calibrating the bar across the team. You take the hardest customer engagement alongside your ICs. You hire people better than you and the team gets sharper every quarter. This role is for someone who is maniacally focused on customer value and who instinctively builds a team that is too. If your team's customers cannot point to time saved, dollars earned, or campaigns shipped, nothing else matters. What you'll do Stay hands-on. Code in the last 6 months — and the next 6. You take the hardest customer engagement alongside your team. You write the reference workflow others learn from. You ship. Hold the room with customers. CMOs, creative directors, heads of IT — and regional execs across geos. You translate tech to ROI and walk away with renewals signed. Performance-manage honestly. Run real 1:1s. Set explicit expectations. Make the hard calls early — promote, redirect, or coach out — and document them. Distinguish underperformance from wrong-fit. Grow the team. Every IC who reports to you should be more capable in 12 months than they were when they joined. Name the specific move you made. If you can't , you're not managing — you're directing. Calibrate the bar. Across customers, across engagements, across the FDCT bench. The work should look the same whether you wrote it or one of your ICs did. Build the playbook. Reusable patterns from your team's engagements become shared repo templates, workflow templates, or upstream workflow nodes. The next pod moves faster because of what your pod learned. Manage vendors and contractors where applicable — calibrate vendor quality, raise the bar when contractor pass-rates slip, rotate out when needed. Feed Adobe product and engineering. Bring real field signal back. Influence what gets built next based on what your team is actually hitting . What you'll bring 8-12 years straddling creative production AND technical building. You have shipped both: real creative work (motion, design, video, 3D, photography, brand systems) AND real technical work (apps, integrations, plugins, automation). The FDCT bar applies to you — at depth. Production code in the last 6 months. If you've drifted into pure people management, this is not your role. Management craft, with evidence. You have hired ICs, run PIPs, made promotion calls that stuck, navigated cross-functional conflict, coached someone out, and grown at least 2–3 ICs who are now more senior because of your management. Names, stories, and specific moves on request. For India M50: Meta-management + cross-geo fluency. You have hired and developed at least 2 managers underneath you. You have recovered from at least one wrong management hire. You have run teams across geos (India + US, ideally EMEA too) and understand the real cost of bad cross-geo coordination. Creative content supply chain fluency. You understand how briefs become assets, how brand systems and design tokens work, how master files become channel variants, how creative teams hand off through Figma / PSD / INDD to DAM and review-and-approval. You read your team's customer engagements at architect depth. Firefly Services + Workflow Builder fluency. Adobe ecosystem depth (AEM, Workfront, Frame.io, App Builder) and Figma plugin / API fluency are strong pluses. You know what the field is hitting before your ICs tell you. Vibe-coding with judgment. Daily AI-tooling use (Cursor / Claude Code / Copilot). You set the AI-native bar for your team — name the tools you use, the wins, and the things AI got wrong that you caught. Extreme ownership and a GSD attitude. When the strategic customer's deal is at risk, you don't

title: Software Development Engineer 2 -  C++
description: At Adobe, we’re passionate about empowering people to create impactful digital experiences. Our Creative Cloud products help millions of designers, animators, and storytellers bring their ideas to life. Adobe Bridge is a powerful creative asset manager that lets you preview, organise, edit and publish multiple creative assets quickly and easily. Why This Role is Exciting Contribute to the development of core Adobe Bridge features, working directly with high-quality, cross-platform C++ code. Leverage AI-assisted and agentic coding tools to accelerate development, review outputs and refine code effectively. Collaborate with cross-functional teams including Product, UX, and QA to implement features that impact millions of users. Gain hands-on experience applying AI tools to enhance productivity, improve code quality, and optimize engineering workflows. What You’ll Do Design, develop, and maintain components of Adobe Bridge. Debug and resolve technical issues across the product. Participate in code reviews and contribute to maintaining engineering standards. Apply AI-assisted workflows to accelerate development, automate repetitive tasks, and generate high-quality code. Improve application performance, reliability, and maintainability. What You Need to Succeed Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science or related field (or equivalent experience). 2+ years of relevant software development experience. Strong C++ programming skills with modern C++ practices. Solid understanding of object-oriented programming, data structures, and algorithms. Knowledge of application development on multiple platforms including Windows and macOS. Strong grasp of operating system fundamentals. Strong problem-solving, debugging, and analytical skills. Familiarity with AI-assisted development tools, including agentic coding workflows, with the ability to direct AI sessions and integrate AI-generated code effectively. What Makes You Stand Out Experience working with desktop applications. Familiarity with graphics, animation, or creative software workflows. Exposure to multithreading, memory management, or performance optimization. Experience working in mature codebases. Hands-on experience using AI tools to assist coding to accelerate feature delivery. title: Principal Enterprise Architect Professional Services (Madrid)
description: The opportunity From the moment you wake up in the morning until you go to bed at night consider the media you consume, the adverts you see, the apps you use, the websites you browse and almost all of the shopping you do online throughout the day. Chances are that every single one of those interactions, every single one of those experiences, was touched by an Adobe product. We have a fantastic opportunity for a Principal Enterprise Architect Professional Services to join our team based in Madrid . As a Principal Enterprise Architect Professional Services , you will guide architecture through the transformation lifecycle. You will build executive confidence, govern design decisions, lead architecture build, and make sure technology choices support long-term value. What you’ll do You will bridge business goals and technical delivery by designing target architectures, planning transitions, and creating decommissioning strategies. Using change management frameworks such as ADKAR, you'll support adoption and sustainability. Key Responsibilities Assess and advance the architectural intent crafted during pre-sales. Provide architectural leadership across the full transformation lifecycle and guide platform adoption and evolution. Convert the success roadmap created during pre-sales into a comprehensive, managed transition plan featuring clear sequencing, decision points, and risk controls. Formulate robust, industry-specific architectural perspectives and translate them into target-state architecture, reference models, and phased implementation plans. Foster and maintain executive trust in architectural direction, serving as a strategic advisor to senior business and technology collaborators. Collaborate with licensing and services sales teams to improve major sales initiatives. Coordinate architectural efforts among delivery teams, subject matter experts, and partners to ensure build integrity and alignment during execution. Establish and lead architecture governance and assurance forums. Partner closely with pre‑sales teams to ensure lessons learned, build patterns, and reference architectures continually improve future pursuits and delivery outcomes. What you’ll need to succeed Proven experience providing enterprise architecture leadership across large-scale transformation programmes, from early visioning and pre-sales through delivery, adoption, and platform evolution. Strong grounding in TOGAF principles and lifecycle-based architecture governance, applied pragmatically in complex enterprise environments. Broad understanding of digital marketing, data, and activation ecosystems. The ability to craft compelling narratives that translate architectural choices into business-relevant results and messages suitable for executives. Experience operating as a trusted architectural advisor to senior executives. Strong understanding of AI, automation, and emerging agentic models, and their implications for enterprise platforms. Experience working closely with delivery teams, partners, and vendors to maintain build integrity. Ability to apply structured change and adoption approaches (including ADKAR) to support the human and organisational aspects of architectural evolution. A collaborative, outcome-focused leadership style, with a relentless focus on value and adoption. Fluency in English and Spanish is required. Any other european language would be a plus. As our many awards will tell you, at Adobe you’ll be immersed in an exceptional work environment that is recognized around the world. You’ll be surrounded by colleagues who are committed to helping each other grow through our unique Check-In approach where ongoing feedback flows freely. If you’re looking to make an impact, Adobe's the place for you. title: Director, Product Marketing- Mobile
description: The Opportunity Creators are Adobe’s next growth engine and together this newly-formed team is re-imagining how millions ideate, edit, and share content. As the Director of Product Marketing for Imaging in our Creators segment , you will lead the strategy and execution that keeps Adobe the go-to destination for creators who are transforming visual storytelling through photography, compositing, and design . This role is pivotal in shaping the future of Adobe’s image editing products, including Photoshop and Lightroom, and driving growth across new creator segments. You’ll partner deeply with Product, Design, Engineering, Research, and GTM teams to define product strategy, launch breakthrough marketing initiatives, and deliver exceptional customer experiences. What You’ll Do Strategic Leadership • Define and implement the long-term product marketing strategy for Adobe’s image editing portfolio for creators . • Influence product direction through deep customer insights, competitive analysis, and market trends—especially around AI and mobile-first workflows. • Frame reasons, positioning, and packaging for new capabilities and offerings. Customer & Market Insights • Become Adobe’s authority on image editing workflows, creator problems, and emerging trends. • Lead qualitative and quantitative research efforts to uncover actionable insights that shape product and marketing strategies. Growth & Go-to-Market Execution • Develop and implement data-driven strategies to acquire new creators, drive activation, and increase engagement and retention. • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver compelling product launches, campaigns, and lifecycle marketing programs. Team Leadership • Build, mentor, and inspire a high-performing team of product marketers. • Foster a culture of innovation, collaboration, and customer obsession. Cross-Functional Influence • Align senior collaborators across Product, Design, Engineering, and GTM teams. • Drive crisp execution across complex, matrixed organizations. Thought Leadership & Storytelling • Champion Adobe’s vision for image editing and creative empowerment. • Craft compelling narratives that resonate with executives, press, partners, and the creator community. What You’ll Need to Succeed 12+ years of experience in product marketing, growth, or related roles—or equivalent practical experience. 6+ years of experience leading high-performing teams. Proven success in consumer or creator technology sectors, ideally with experience in image editing, photography, or design tools. Proven track record scaling a major mobile app BS degree in Marketing, Business, or a related field—or equivalent practical experience. Strong critical thinking and analytical skills, with the ability to translate insights into action. Exceptional communication and storytelling abilities. Demonstrated ability to influence senior leaders and drive cross-functi onal alignment. Passion for creators and a deep understanding of their needs and workflows. title: Senior Manager, Employee Brand & Communications
description: The Opportunity Employee Brand & Communications is a fast-paced team responsible for internal communications that align and activate employees as confident brand, culture, and product advocates. We are a forward-thinking team of communicators who build trust, product proficiency, and pride from the inside out. We are seeking a Senior Manager, Employee Communications, to lead internal communications related to Finance, Legal, and IT. This highly valued role will partner closely with functions including Legal, Privacy, Cybersecurity, Finance, and IT to deliver global and targeted employee communications. This role will impact how we inform, educate, and guide employees on cybersecurity risks and practices, critical policies, regulatory requirements, technology changes, and data protection practices. The ideal candidate will have a history of impactful employee communications experience and be a strong writer and communications advisor who can translate complex legal and technical topics into actionable messaging for a global employee audience. What you'll Do Develop and implement comprehensive internal communications strategies supporting Legal, Privacy, Finance, Cybersecurity, and IT programs. Partner closely across the Employee Communications team to ensure connection and alignment. Serve as a trusted communications partner and advisor to senior leaders including the Chief Information Officer, Chief Security Officer, and Chief Privacy Officer. Work as a close partner across our communications ecosystem, including regional communications, executive communications, and public relations. Anticipate reputational, cultural, and employee trust implications of sensitive topics and guide messaging accordingly. Own the development and execution of communications plans, and transform complex technical information into clear, actionable communications. Partner with Cybersecurity and Privacy teams to develop and manage incident response communications for security incidents. Lead creative, compelling employee awareness campaigns on cybersecurity and risk mitigation. Build clear, effective messaging and change communications for Finance and IT initiatives. Maintain a communications roadmap for these functions and build communication practices and processes. What you need to succeed 8–10 years of experience in internal communications, corporate communications, or a related field. Experience supporting Legal, Security, Privacy, Compliance, Finance, or regulated environments preferred. Exceptional communication capabilities, both written and verbal, with the ability to clearly articulate complex, nuanced issues. Experience advising executive leaders at senior levels. Excellent judgment in high-risk, confidential situations. Experience with crisis communications and incident response. Innovative thinking and the capacity to take risks and push Adobe in new directions! Inherent curiosity, with an appetite for an exciting opportunity on a collaborative team! Capacity to thrive in a fast-paced environment. title: Forward Deployment Engineer
description: The Team You will be joining the newly formed Forward Deployment Engineering (FDE) team within Adobe’s Digital Experience organization. As part of a key innovation initiative, our mandate is to bridge the gap between product engineering and real-world implementation. We are a team of world-class engineers dedicated to solving the most complex challenges for the world's largest companies, ensuring they extract maximum value from the Adobe ecosystem. The Opportunity This is a chance to join a "startup within a giant." As a Forward Deployment Engineer, you will be the hands-on builder bridging the gap between Adobe’s core products and real-world customer needs. You won’t just be writing tickets in a backlog; you will be onsite (or virtual) with customer engineering teams, rapidly prototyping solutions, and writing the code that connects the Adobe Experience Cloud to the world’s largest enterprises. If you love to hack on complex problems, ship code fast, and see immediate impact, this is your team. What You'll Do Build and Ship Fast: Rapidly design and implement features and integrations based on immediate customer requirements, moving quickly from "whiteboard sketch" to working code. AI-Driven Prototyping: Experiment with Generative AI and agentic workflows to create novel demos and automation tools that showcase the future of the Adobe ecosystem. Production-Grade Engineering: Write clean, efficient, and testable Java/Scala code for high-availability systems and RESTful APIs. Solve the Puzzle: Troubleshoot complex integration issues in real-time, performing root cause analysis when services don't behave as expected. Collaborate & Learn: Partner with senior architects to understand the "why" behind the build, while providing feedback to product teams on how customers are actually using our APIs and AI features. What You Bring 3+ years of software engineering experience with strong fundamentals in Java or Scala . Experience building backend services using frameworks like Spring Boot . Familiarity with LLM Development: You have used APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini) to build applications and understand the basics of prompt engineering and context management. Solid understanding of REST API concepts and how to consume/build web services. Practical experience with SQL/NoSQL databases (you know how to write a query and design a basic schema). A "hacker" mindset: You are curious, you learn new technologies quickly, and you enjoy finding creative workarounds to technical blockers. Bonus Points (Desired Extras) We are looking for well-rounded engineers who love technology. Any of the following are a huge plus: Agentic Frameworks: Experience playing with LangChain , Semantic Kernel, or AutoGPT concepts to build autonomous agents. RAG Knowledge: Understanding of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (vector databases, embeddings) to ground LLM responses in real data. Full-Stack Curiosity: Familiarity with frontend frameworks (React, Angular, or Vue) to build complete end-to-end demos. Side Projects & Hackathons: A GitHub profile showing personal projects—especially those involving AI wrappers, chatbots, or automation tools . Cloud Playground: Experience deploying your own apps to AWS, Azure, or GCP (e.g., deploying a Python AI bot on Lambda). Data Tinkering: Basic exposure to data streaming (Kafka) or big data tools; you aren't afraid of large datasets. title: Director, Global Event Security
description: The Opportunity Adobe is hiring a Director of Event Security to lead security strategy, planning, and implementation for all corporate events worldwide! This includes product launches, developer conferences, executive summits, and employee gatherings. You will ensure that every event offers a secure and seamless experience for attendees, executives, employees, and partners. The role bridges physical security, executive protection, crisis management, and collaboration across departments. You will convert complex and evolving threats into scalable, practical security programs that protect our people, brand, and intellectual property globally. You will guide a high-performing event security team while collaborating closely with Events, Marketing, Legal, Facilities, IT, and senior leadership to embed security throughout event planning and delivery. As the foremost expert in event security at the company, you will set the standard for event protection—monitoring industry leaders, adopting new technologies, and continuously enhancing our approach! What you'll Do Lead and continue to build the global event security program, including strategy, policies, standards, and playbooks across regions and event types. Assess event risk, considering geopolitical context, venue conditions, crowd dynamics, and reputational risk. Plan and oversee security for corporate events, including site surveys, access control, credentialing, emergency response plans, and communications. Oversee on‑site security operations, working closely with internal teams and external partners to deliver safe, well‑executed events. Coordinate executive protection needs with the Protective Operations team for high‑profile events and keynotes. Develop and maintain event‑specific incident response and crisis procedures, including medical, environmental, and security threats. Act as a trusted security partner to Events, Marketing, Facilities, Legal, IT, and Executive teams throughout the event lifecycle. Manage third‑party security providers and event security budgets to support efficient and scalable execution. What you need to succeed Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience. Senior-level experience in physical or corporate security with clear ownership of event security programs. Demonstrated experience securing corporate events across multiple regions and regulatory environments, up to 20,000+ attendees. Proven ability to plan and manage security operations for events of varying size and complexity. Working knowledge of access control, surveillance systems, and incident management tools. Experience managing third‑party security vendors and on‑site security teams. Strong incident response and crisis decision‑making skills. Executive presence Willingness to travel and work flexible hours during events. title: Senior Finance Manager
description: The Opportunity Join Adobe’s world-class FP&A organization as a Senior Finance Manager. You will act as a strategic finance partner to the Adobe Technology Services (ATS) organization. In this highly visible role, you will lead financial planning and analysis efforts and craft how Adobe manages and optimizes its enterprise software investments, including some of the most innovative and rapidly evolving AI technologies in the market. This role combines finance, technology, and strategy, providing an outstanding chance to impact executive decisions. You will enhance operational performance and support scaling Adobe’s next-generation AI and software ecosystem. The position offers a fast-paced, high-growth setting with direct access to senior leaders and excellent career growth opportunities. What you'll Do Key Responsibilities: ATS Finance Leadership Serve as a trusted advisor to ATS leadership, delivering actionable insights to inform strategic decisions and drive business outcomes Lead end-to-end forecasting, budgeting, and financial reporting for the ATS organization Coordinate monthly and quarterly close processes in partnership with the India FP&A team, including variance analysis, risk and opportunity identification, and headcount reporting Drive the annual operating plan (AOP), including scenario modeling, investment prioritization, and cross-functional alignment Develop and deliver clear, compelling executive-level presentations and financial narratives that influence senior collaborators Key Responsibilities: Enterprise Software & AI Strategy Take ownership of and develop reporting for Adobe’s enterprise software portfolio, covering spend management, usage trends, vendor concentration, and risk insights Lead financial oversight of strategic AI tools and platforms (e.g., Cursor, ChatGPT, Claude), tracking adoption, cost efficiency, and value realization Strengthen the operating model across Finance, ATS, and Procurement for new software agreements, renewals, and commercial negotiations Evaluate and recommend efficient cost allocation models (e.g., centralization vs. chargeback) to improve transparency, accountability, and scalability What you need to succeed Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, Economics, or a related field. 10+ years of FP&A or related financial analysis experience. Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint. Strong verbal and written communication skills with the ability to simplify complex concepts. High attention to detail, strong organizational skills, and a collaborative attitude that aims to address challenges constructively. Ability to thrive in a fast‑paced, dynamic environment and prioritize various tasks effectively. Nice to have: CPA, MBA, and experience with Anaplan, Power BI, Tableau, or SAP. title: Senior / Information Retrieval Engineer (AI/ML), Brand Concierge
description: The Opportunity We are seeking a highly skilled Information Retrieval Engineer to lead the development and optimization of retrieval systems that power context-aware large language models (LLMs). This role focuses on building robust Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines to ensure AI agents and applications have access to the most relevant, timely, and high-quality information. You'll work at the intersection of data engineering, machine learning, and knowledge management—enabling better reasoning, accuracy, and performance for enterprise-grade AI systems. What you'll Do RAG System Design Architect and deploy scalable retrieval pipelines using vector databases (e.g., FAISS, Weaviate, Pinecone, Qdrant) Implement semantic search infrastructure and hybrid retrieval systems (semantic + keyword) Data Processing & Ingestion Build ingestion pipelines for both structured and unstructured data sources Implement document chunking strategies, embedding generation (e.g., OpenAI, Cohere, HuggingFace), and metadata tagging Retrieval Optimization Fine-tune relevance scoring, reranking algorithms, and query understanding mechanisms Develop techniques to improve precision/recall for specific business domains or user tasks Knowledge Enhancement Create and maintain knowledge graphs to support context linking and disambiguation Manage data freshness and version control to ensure consistency and reliability of retrieved content Reasoning Support Design and iterate on context window strategies that improve LLM reasoning (e.g., adaptive injection, task-based retrieval) Collaborate with prompt engineers and model developers to align retrieval outputs with downstream model behavior Performance Monitoring Track key retrieval metrics such as accuracy, latency, and fallback rate Implement caching, prefetching, and deduplication strategies to optimize system responsiveness What you need to succeed 4+ years in data engineering, ML infrastructure, or information retrieval Experience building and deploying RAG pipelines or semantic search systems Strong ML and Python skills and familiarity with retrieval libraries (e.g., Haystack, LangChain, Elasticsearch, Milvus) Proficiency with embedding models, vector similarity search, and document indexing Familiarity with cloud platforms and MLOps tooling (e.g., Airflow, dbt, Docker) Preferred Qualifications Knowledge of graph databases (e.g., Neo4j, TigerGraph) or knowledge graph design Experience optimizing retrieval for LLMs (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral) Background This role is designed for a senior adoption leader with AI gravitas and deep passion —someone who can confidently advise executives on how AI reshapes operating models, decision-making, governance, and workforce capabilities , not just how tools are deployed. The Principal Adoption Digital Strategist operates as a trusted advisor to C-suite and senior leadership , shaping adoption strategy across multi-year, multi-workstream transformations. The ideal candidate combines strategic depth with delivery credibility and is comfortable navigating ambiguity, scale, and complexity especially for AI programs. title: Director, Marketing Finance
description: POSITION SUMMARY This Finance Marketing Director position is a strategic finance leader supporting the Global Marketing Organization’s (GMO) Customer Marketing Group, with primary focus on the Creativity & Productivity (C&P) marketing organization. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES 1. Data-Informed Decision Making & Strategic Business Partnership Provide strategic finance leadership for the GMO VP of Customer Marketing, supporting the C&P marketing organization (Business Professionals & Consumers and Creators & Creative Professionals teams). Serve as a proactive thought partner to marketing leadership, shifting discussions from reporting results to understanding drivers, implications, and actions. Guide cross-functional alignment of investment priorities and strategic decisions across Product Marketing, Go-to-Market, GMO, and Finance leadership teams. Deliver recurring business reviews and executive reporting that translate financial and marketing trends into clear business implications and recommended actions. 2. Marketing Effectiveness & Investment Optimization Proactively identify diminishing returns, underperforming investments, and budget reallocation opportunities to improve marketing value and investment efficiency. Partner closely with Product Marketing and Growth Marketing Insights (GMI) teams to evaluate performance trends and optimize marketing investments across the funnel. Build and maintain an executive-level Marketing Return on Investment scorecard, providing leadership with ongoing insight into program performance and spend efficiency. 3. Budget Management & Financial Planning Own end-to-end financial management of variable marketing budget (>$1B annually), lead quarterly forecast cycles, risk and opportunity assessments, and scenario planning. Establish investment guardrails, approval workflows, and budget shifts that balance financial control with the agility modern marketing demands. 4. Leadership & Team Development Lead and develop a high-performing finance team, focused analytical rigor, business partnership, and continuous improvement. Represent marketing finance in cross-functional leadership forums, including the Finance Leadership Team, GMO and C&P marketing leadership meetings. QUALIFICATIONS & EXPERIENCE Required 10+ years of progressive finance experience, with a strong background in topline product/go-to-market finance and/or marketing finance role within a consumer-facing business. Demonstrated expertise evaluating marketing efficiency, including experience with multi-channel investment analysis, attribution methodologies, and/or media mix modeling. Proven track record leading new financial capabilities, processes, and ways of working within marketing finance and/or FP&A environments. Advanced financial modeling skills; proficiency in Excel and planning tools (e.g., Anaplan, SAP). Experience with BI and visualization tools (e.g. PowerBI). Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or a related quantitative field, or equivalent experience. description: Adobe Firefly’s Generative AI Services team is seeking a Principal Service Engineer to serve as the technical lead for our GenAI Services domain. In this high-impact role, you will lead a team of talented engineers in building scalable, high-performance generative AI systems—powering features across Adobe products such as Firefly, Photoshop, Illustrator, Express, Stock, Premiere, and our suite of GenAI Enterprise solutions. You will design and develop APIs and systems that integrate both Adobe’s proprietary and third-party generative models. As a hands-on technical leader, you will champion technical excellence and operational rigor while productizing and scaling a rapidly growing portfolio of GenAI services. title: Principal Applied AI Product Manager
description: The Opportunity The GTM AI & Agent ic Products team is leading the shift to an Agentic operating model for Adobe GTM and Sales. This function is the orchestration layer that operationalizes Adobe GTM and Sales strategy into end to end Agentic AI workflows that compress planning cycles from months to weeks, launch more personalized campaigns across every route to market, and streamline manual tasks so sellers can focus on talking to customers. We are building an always-on Agentic operating layer that connects strategy, data, and frontline execution for the Digital Media business. The AI industry is changing quickly, so success in this role requires first principles thinking, intellectual curiosity, and the ability to adapt as the landscape evolves. This opportunity is ideal for a Principal Product Manager who brings a founder approach, enjoys building zero to one products , and has the grit to see them through to impact. Role Overview Agentic Planning Define the product vision and strategy for Planning Agents that support annual and in year planning across multiple products, segments, and regions. Build Agentic workflows that propose territories, coverage models, capacity plans, quotas, and targeted subgroup plays from live data and clear business constraints. Embed these Planning Agents into existing planning cadences so leaders can review, adjust, and approve plans instead of starting from scratch . Campaign Orchestration and Execution Own the roadmap for Execution Agents that help GTM teams select audiences, prioritize accounts, choose plays, and orchestrate campaigns across key channels and tools. Partner with marketing, sales development, and Sales teams to codify successful campaigns into reusable Agentic workflows that can be adapted by different segments and regions. Build a standard experimentation framework so campaigns can be launched in days, tested with clear hypotheses and metrics, and scaled when they work. Seller Productivity and AI Assistants Define and deliver seller facing AI assistants that support account research, meeting preparation, opportunity insights, next best actions, and follow-up content. Map complete seller workflows and identify where Agents can remove friction, from territory and account review to renewal and expansion motions. Partner with Sales Systems to embed assistants into CRM, email, calendar, and other daily tools, so they fit naturally into seller workflows. What you will bring 8+ years of product management (2+ years of Agentic A I ) experience, with meaningful work in one or more of the following: sales planning, GTM systems, marketing technology, sales tools, or AI powered products. Experience mapping complex business workflows and translating them into clear product requirements and step by step experiences. Comfort working with data and experimentation, including defining success metrics, reviewing analyses, and using insights to drive roadmap decisions. Solid understanding of modern AI technologies such as large language models and agentic workflows, or a demonstrable ability to ramp quickly. A builder mentality, high ownership, and resilience in a fast moving environment. Technical requirements Experience using SQL to explore data, validate hypotheses, and define requirements with analytics partners. Experience with LLM and agentic architectures, such as prompt design, retrieval augmented generation, tool calling, and evaluation. Experience partnering with engineers on API design, data contracts, and integration patterns between systems. Proficiency with product and design tools including Figma, FigJam , Miro, or similar for mapping workflows and defining user experiences. Experience using product delivery tools such as Jira, Confluence, or similar to manage backlogs, write clear tickets, and track progress. You will lead executive-level collaboration, guide digital transformation governance, and promote the strategic value of Adobe’s enterprise programs. In this senior individual contributor role, you will advise C-suite partners and VPs, ensuring Adobe’s platform vision, transformation plan, and Centers of Excellence (CoE) models deliver measurable results for customers. We are seeking a Principal Project Manager who can provide strategic oversight, executive governance, and multi-workstream coordination for Adobe’s most critical customers. Drive end-to-end program leadership , orchestrating multi-workstream delivery across Experience Cloud solutions, data platforms, content supply chain, and digital experience operations. Monitor and report on program performance, KPIs, and business value impact, ensuring clear visibility to customer leadership and Adobe stakeholders. Ensure operational excellence through strong oversight of schedules, budgets, dependencies, risks, and delivery quality across all engagements. Drive account growth by identifying opportunities for additional services and value creation. Collaborate with Product, Engineering, and Global Services teams to ensure customer needs are anticipated and addressed with the right solutions and delivery approach. title: Solution Account Manager

We’re passionate about empowering people to develop and transform how companies engage with their customers across every touchpoint. The Challenge Within the Digital Experience organization, you will be a Solution Account Manager (SAM) responsible for managing a portfolio of existing customers. You will act as the primary contact across all stages of the customer lifecycle, including onboarding, adoption, value realization, renewal, and growth. You will operate in close alignment with Account Executives, Renewal Sales Specialists, Product Specialists, and Customer Success teams to ensure customers realize continuous value from Adobe solutions. What You’ll Do Account & Customer Lifecycle Management Own the customer journey from onboarding and implementation to value realization and renewal, serving as the main point of contact for any critical issues in your accounts. title: Senior Data Engineer & Architect
description: Senior Data Engineer & Architect Location: San Jose, CA Job Type: Full-Time Reporting to: Senior Manager, Data & Analytics Platform Engineering About the Role The Adobe Experience Platform (AEP) Product Success Engineering team is hiring a Senior Data Engineer & Architect to help build and scale the infrastructure that powers product adoption insights, customer retention analytics, system performance monitoring, and operational intelligence across Adobe. In this role, you will design and build reliable, scalable, cloud-native data systems while contributing to platform-wide architectural direction and standards. You’ll collaborate closely with Data Scientists, Analytics Engineers, Solution Architects, and Product partners to translate evolving business needs into durable data solutions that support analytics and machine learning use cases. This opportunity is ideal for someone who enjoys hands-on engineering while influencing broader data architecture strategy. What You’ll Do Design & Evolve the Data Platform Partner with engineering leadership to develop and evolve enterprise-scale data architecture across warehouses, lakes, and modern data platforms. Develop logical and physical data models crafted to enable analytics and ML use cases, including dimensional models and optimized schemas. Contribute to platform standards, reference architectures, and documentation across ingestion, transformation, governance, and analytics enablement. Collaborate with Enterprise Architecture and Cloud Infrastructure teams to align with security, compliance, performance, and cost optimization guidelines. Build Scalable Data Pipelines Design, develop, and maintain reliable ETL/ELT pipelines integrating data from usage, clickstream, entitlement, cost, and support systems. Build modular, reusable pipeline frameworks using orchestration tools such as Airflow, Dagster, or Prefect. Optimize data processing workflows for performance, scalability, and cost efficiency using distributed systems (e.g., Spark, Beam). Work with Data Scientists and Analytics Engineers to operationalize data products and ML-ready datasets. Strengthen Governance, Quality & Reliability Design and implement scalable frameworks for managing and improving data integrity, including cataloging, lineage tracking, access controls, and automated validation processes to ensure trusted, production-grade data. Establish and continuously improve reliability standards for data services, defining and tracking key SLOs such as freshness, pipeline success rates, and availability through proactive monitoring and alerting. Maintain clear metadata, documentation, and data definitions to improve discoverability, enable self-service analytics, and support consistent operational excellence across the platform. What You’ll Bring Experience Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field — or equivalent practical experience. 8+ years of experience in data engineering or software engineering, including building and operating production data platforms. Experience contributing to data architecture decisions at the system or platform level. Technical Expertise Strong experience with cloud data warehouses (e.g., Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift), including schema design and performance tuning. Experience building production-grade pipelines using orchestration frameworks (Airflow, Dagster, Prefect) and distributed processing tools (Spark, Beam). Deep understanding of ETL/ELT patterns, dimensional modeling, and analytics dataset design. Experience implementing data governance and quality practices, including lineage, validation, and role-based access controls. Proficiency in Scale or Java, plus experience Python & SQL Experience working with at least one major cloud platform (AWS, Azure, or GCP). Experience defining data modeling standards, naming conventions, and data contracts. Familiarity with event-driven and streaming systems (e.g., Kafka, Kinesis). Communication & Collaboration Ability to translate business questions into scalable data models and platform capabilities. Ability to clearly explain data and architectural concepts to diverse audiences. Nice to Have Experience in B2B SaaS, product analytics, or customer success environments. Hands-on experience with modern data stack tools (dbt, Great Expectations, Atlan, Collibra). Exposure to MLOps concepts such as feature pipelines and training datasets. Why Join This Team Develop the architecture of a critical data platform
